Missed GT:
  • MHC class II deficiency, also known as Bare Lymphocyte Syndrome (BLS), is a rare combined immunodeficiency caused by mutations in genes regulating expression of MHCII molecules.
  • MHCII deficiency results in impaired cellular and humoral immune responses, leading to severe infections and autoimmunity.
  • In MHCII−/− mice, impaired lymphostromal cross-talk in the thymus affects medullary thymic epithelial cell (mTEC) maturation and promiscuous gene expression and causes defects of central tolerance.
  • DOI 10.3389/fimmu.2021.669943 identifies the Ferrua et al. study on MHCII deficiency and thymic epithelial cell alterations.
  • Absence of MHCII expression causes abnormal cross-talk with developing T cells, likely leading to defects in thymic epithelial cells (TEC).

src 6 · Winship study links survivorship care to longer life for childhood ... · hits 4/12 · bl 0 · emitted 17
EmittedMatched GTScore
A study led by researchers at Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta found that childhood cancer survivors who engage in long-term survivorship programs and receive a personalized survivorship care plan live longer than childhood cancer survivors who do not receive those services.(no-match)-
The research was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.(no-match)-
The study was co-led by Sharon M. Castellino, MD, MSc, who is a professor and pediatric oncologist at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta and a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.(no-match)-
The study’s lead author was Xu Ji, PhD, MSPH, who is a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute and an associate professor in the Department of Pediatrics at Emory University School of Medicine and the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta.(no-match)-
The research team followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors who were diagnosed between 2002 and 2016 and were eligible for a survivorship program at a comprehensive pediatric cancer center.(no-match)-
The study tracked the 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors for up to 10 years.(no-match)-
Just over half of the study participants, 55.9%, received a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
✓ A survivorship care plan is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history and outlines recommended screenings, late-effect monitoring, and healthy-living guidance.A survivorship care plan (SCP) is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history and outlines recommended screenings, late-effect monitoring, and healthy-living guidance.0.935
✓ Children and adolescent cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan had a 38% lower risk of death over 10 years than survivors who did not receive a survivorship care plan.Childhood cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes than survivors who never received a plan.0.902
Survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits than survivors with fewer survivorship visits or fewer updates.(no-match)-
Survivorship care plan recipients were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes than survivors who never received a plan.(no-match)-
The study is described as the first large, contemporary, and diverse cohort study to link survivorship care plan receipt within a pediatric cancer survivorship program to improved survival.(no-match)-
✓ Researchers found that older survivors, non-Hispanic Black survivors, and survivors with central nervous system tumors or surgery-only treatment were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.Older childhood cancer survivors, those identifying as non-Hispanic Black, those with central nervous system tumors, and those who received surgery-only treatment were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.0.927
The research team plans to continue tracking survivors beyond 10 years.(no-match)-
The research team plans to examine quality of life alongside survival in future studies.(no-match)-
The research team plans to explore web-based platforms for helping survivors and clinicians access survivorship care plans nationwide.(no-match)-
✓ Cancer SurvivorLink is a web-based platform developed by coauthor Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, who is a professor and co-director of the Aflac Survivor Program at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta and a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.Cancer SurvivorLink is a web-based platform developed by Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, to help survivors and clinicians access survivorship care plans.0.903
Missed GT:
  • A Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children's Healthcare of Atlanta study found that childhood cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan had a 38% lower risk of death over 10 years compared with those who did not.
  • The survivorship care plan study followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016 for up to 10 years.
  • 55.9% of patients in the Winship childhood cancer survivorship study received a survivorship care plan (SCP).
  • Xu Ji, PhD, MSPH, is a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute and associate professor in the Department of Pediatrics at Emory University School of Medicine.
  • Sharon M. Castellino, MD, MSc, is a professor and pediatric oncologist at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta.
  • The Winship survivorship-care-plan study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.
  • Childhood cancer survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits.
  • Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, is a professor and co-director of the Aflac Survivor Program at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta and a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.

  • Ethical issues often hinder the practical implementation of LLM mitigation strategies, particularly in high-stake areas like healthcare and public governance.
  • Existing frameworks for LLM ethics often lack adaptability, failing to accommodate evolving societal expectations and diverse contexts.
  • Mitigation strategies for ethical challenges in Large Language Models are complex and domain-dependent.

  • In Advaita Vedanta, the term 'Advaita' translates to 'non-duality', emphasizing the indivisible unity of all existence.
  • Advaita Vedanta holds that Brahman (ultimate reality) and Atman (individual self) are fundamentally identical.
  • The Upanishadic mahavakya 'Tat Tvam Asi' ('Thou art That') articulates the identity of Atman and Brahman in Advaita Vedanta.
  • Avidya (ignorance) in Advaita Vedanta is said to veil the true nature of the self and cause the perception of separation between Atman and Brahman.
  • Maya in Advaita Vedanta is the cosmic illusion that creates the appearance of duality and multiplicity in the world.
  • In Advaita Vedanta, through Maya, the unchanging Brahman appears as the dynamic, ever-changing phenomenal world.
  • Advaita Vedanta distinguishes three levels of reality: Paramarthika (absolute), Vyavaharika (empirical), and Prabhasika (illusory).
  • Moksha in Advaita Vedanta is liberation attained by realizing the non-dual nature of Brahman through jnana (knowledge).
  • In Advaita Vedanta, liberation is aided through practices such as meditation and study of scriptures, which help transcend ignorance.
  • Sakshin in Advaita Vedanta is the witnessing consciousness that remains untouched by the mind-body complex.

src 11 · Hybrid Architectures for Language Models: Systematic Analysis and ... · hits 0/4 · bl 0 · emitted 7

The paper "Hybrid Architectures for Language Models: Systematic Analysis and Design Insights" presents a comprehensive evaluation of hybrid language models that combine self-attention with structured state space models.(no-match)-
The paper describes structured state space models such as Mamba as components of hybrid language model architectures.(no-match)-
The paper states that hybrid architectures can achieve a balance between modeling quality and computational efficiency, especially for long-context tasks.(no-match)-
The paper analyzes inter-layer fusion strategies, also called sequential fusion, and intra-layer fusion strategies, also called parallel fusion, for hybrid architectures.(no-match)-
The paper evaluates hybrid architectures from the perspectives of language modeling performance, long-context capabilities, scaling analysis, and training and inference efficiency.(no-match)-
The paper identifies the most critical elements for each hybridization strategy by investigating the core characteristics of their computational primitive.(no-match)-
The paper proposes optimal design recipes for both hybrid models and provides practical guidance for developing hybrid language models.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The paper 'Hybrid Architectures for Language Models' evaluates language models that combine self-attention mechanisms with structured state space models like Mamba.
  • Hybrid language-model architectures aim to balance modeling quality and computational efficiency, particularly for long-context tasks.
  • The hybrid language-model study analyzes two fusion strategies: inter-layer (sequential) and intra-layer (parallel).
  • The hybrid language-model paper evaluates designs along language-modeling performance, long-context capability, scaling, and training and inference efficiency.

The article states that questions have arisen about whether the nature of a task muddles an evaluation of true reasoning.(no-match)-
The article identifies the requirement that a model keep track of the state space on its own as one potential confound in reasoning evaluation.(no-match)-
The authors provided a large language model with an environment interface for Tower of Hanoi problems.(no-match)-
The environment interface allowed the large language model to make a move with a tool call, provide written justification, observe the resulting state space, and reprompt itself for the next move.(no-match)-
The authors observed that access to an environment interface did not delay or eradicate performance collapse.(no-match)-
LLM-parameterized policy analysis revealed increasing divergence from both optimal policies and uniformly random policies.(no-match)-
The authors conclude that the model exhibits mode-like collapse at each level of complexity.(no-match)-
The authors suggest that performance depends on whether the mode reflects the correct solution for the problem.(no-match)-

Missed GT:
  • Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) were reported to undergo a collapse in performance on solving puzzles beyond certain perplexity thresholds.
  • The arXiv 2510.15974 study gave an LLM an environment interface for Tower of Hanoi problems, allowing tool-call moves with written justifications.
  • The arXiv 2510.15974 study found that access to an environment interface did not delay or eradicate performance collapse on Tower of Hanoi.
  • The arXiv 2510.15974 study observed increasing divergence of the LLM policy from both optimal and uniformly-random policies as problem complexity grew.
  • The arXiv 2510.15974 study found that LLM performance on Tower of Hanoi is dependent on whether the model's mode reflects the correct solution for the problem.

  • Traditional Chinese Medicine originated over 5,000 years ago and is rooted in the ancient philosophy of Taoism.

  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, acupuncture involves insertion of very thin needles through the skin at points positioned along pathways called meridians.
  • Acupuncture has been shown to have pain-relieving, anti-inflammatory, hormone-regulating, and immunostimulating effects.
  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, acupuncture regulates and restores the normal flow of Qi, restoring health in the process.
  • Meridians in Traditional Chinese Medicine have been shown in recent research to be real, anatomical structures potentially involved in tissue restoration and repair.

A microdiscectomy is commonly performed by a CNSO neurosurgeon or an orthopedic spine surgeon.(no-match)-

✓ The goal of discectomy and microdiscectomy is to alleviate pressure on the spinal cord or on an aggravated, pinched nerve.The goal of a microdiscectomy is to alleviate pressure on the spinal cord or on an aggravated, pinched nerve.0.945

✓ The incision for a minimally invasive microdiscectomy is no more than 2 centimeters.In a minimally invasive microdiscectomy the incision is no more than 2 centimeters.0.967
Dilators may or may not be used during a microdiscectomy to maintain visualization through the small opening.(no-match)-
Spondylosis or arthritis of the spine can create a need for a larger field of visualization during spine surgery.(no-match)-

✓ Most patients can return to work within two weeks after microdiscectomy surgery if their job is not labor intensive.Most patients can return to work within two weeks after microdiscectomy, assuming the job is not labor intensive.0.966
Providers commonly recommend limiting bending, lifting, and twisting movements during the recovery period after a microdiscectomy.(no-match)-

Other studies show that recurrent lumbar disc herniation incidences are in the 5% to 18% range.(no-match)-

Complications after a microdiscectomy can include damage to surrounding tissue, spinal fluid leakage, and adverse anesthesia reactions.(no-match)-

  • In Jainism, fasting falls under tapas or asceticism, which varies by degree between the laity and monastics.
  • Paryushan is a Jain holiday observed annually around August to September, lasting 8 to 10 days.
  • Mircea Eliade was a 20th-century Romanian historian of religion.
  • Mircea Eliade described sacred time as lying outside of ordinary time, and fasting as one way to enter it.
  • Confucians use the term 'ren' to describe a type of wisdom from fasting that translates as humanity, humaneness, goodness, benevolence, or love.
  • Daoism includes the concept of 'fasting of the heart-mind,' which involves detoxing the soul by controlling the five senses.

src 18 · Precision Medicine and Targeted Cancer Therapy · hits 6/14 · bl 0 · emitted 29
EmittedMatched GTScore
The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003.(no-match)-
✓ The Human Genome Project decoded the entire human genome composed of 3 billion DNA base pairs.The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003 and decoded the entire human genome composed of 3 billion DNA base pairs.0.906
The Human Genome Project identified the exome containing approximately 23,000 genes.(no-match)-
The exome is about 2% of the entire DNA content of the genome.(no-match)-
The majority of disease-causing mutations occur in the exome.(no-match)-
✓ In 2015, the US Precision Medicine Initiative placed cancer research and treatment at the forefront of medical priorities in the United States.The US Precision Medicine Initiative launched in 2015 placed cancer research and treatment at the forefront of US medical priorities.0.961
Precision medicine uses genomic information to guide medical decision making.(no-match)-
Targeted therapy is a type of cancer treatment that blocks cancer cell growth by interfering with targeted molecules involved in the development of cancer.(no-match)-
Herceptin has been used in early-stage and late-stage breast cancer treatment.(no-match)-
Herceptin acts on the receptor site for human epidermal growth factor receptor 2, also called Her-2.(no-match)-
The Her-2 pathway promotes growth and cell division when it functions normally.(no-match)-
Overexpression of Her-2 protein on cell membranes can cause unregulated cell growth and tumor formation.(no-match)-
✓ Herceptin blocks Her-2 receptor function and prevents cancer cell proliferation.Herceptin blocks the Her-2 receptor function and prevents cancer cell proliferation.0.994
✓ Her-2 chemotherapy medications may cause cardiotoxicity, especially when they are used with anthracycline chemotherapy agents.Her-2 chemotherapy medications may cause cardiotoxicity, especially when used with anthracycline chemotherapy agents.0.996
Immunotherapy uses the body’s immune system to fight cancer.(no-match)-
✓ Checkpoint inhibitors are monoclonal antibody drugs that bind checkpoint receptor sites on T lymphocytes.Checkpoint inhibitors are monoclonal antibody drugs that bind checkpoint receptor sites on T lymphocytes to allow the immune system to kill tumor cells.0.956
✓ Pembrolizumab, sold under the brand name Keytruda, binds to the PD-1 receptor on T-lymphocytes.Pembrolizumab (brand name Keytruda) is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD-1 (programmed cell death 1) receptor.0.901
Pembrolizumab inhibits the PD-1 pathway that depresses the responsiveness of the immune system.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab has shown effectiveness against lung, bladder, head and neck, renal cell, and Hodgkin lymphoma cancers.(no-match)-
Former US president Jimmy Carter is receiving Keytruda for metastatic melanoma to the brain.(no-match)-
Jimmy Carter’s brain tumor has shrunk and is no longer detectable on imaging studies after use of pembrolizumab.(no-match)-
The first checkpoint inhibitor Yervoy, also called ipilimumab, was released in 2011.(no-match)-
The average monthly cost of pembrolizumab is around $14,500.(no-match)-
Higher doses of pembrolizumab used in clinical trials cost up to $83,000.(no-match)-
Immunotherapy does not work for all people with the same cancers or for all types of cancers.(no-match)-
Tumors with a high number of genetic mutations seem to be susceptible to checkpoint inhibitor activity.(no-match)-
Melanoma and lymphoma have shown consistent susceptibility to checkpoint inhibitors.(no-match)-
The era of precision medicine is expected to improve mortality and morbidity in cancer.(no-match)-
Genomic information of both the individual and the tumor itself will be critically important in medical decision making.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The human exome contains approximately 23,000 genes and represents about 2% of the total DNA content of the genome.
  • Herceptin is a targeted therapy used for early and late stage breast cancer that acts on the human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her-2).
  • Checkpoint inhibitors have been shown to be effective against lung cancer, bladder cancer, head and neck cancer, renal cell cancer, and Hodgkin lymphoma.
  • Cancer cells evade immune system surveillance through a process called immunoediting.
  • Tumors stimulate upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion and produce an immune tolerant environment via cytokine production.
  • Cancer avoids immune system surveillance by creating new surface proteins called neoantigens that are difficult to recognize as foreign, or by reducing antigenic proteins on the cell surface.
  • Cancers that are hypermutated can result from exposure to carcinogens such as tobacco and ultraviolet light.
  • Former US President Jimmy Carter received pembrolizumab (Keytruda) for metastatic melanoma to the brain, and his brain tumor shrunk and became undetectable on imaging after treatment.
src 19 · Seven-Year Experiment Uncovers New Insights into Nature of ... · hits 2/11 · bl 0 · emitted 23
EmittedMatched GTScore
A multinational team of neuroscientists tested the integrated information theory of consciousness and the global neuronal workspace theory of consciousness.(no-match)-
✓ The integrated information theory of consciousness suggests that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.Integrated Information Theory (IIT) posits that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.0.911
The global neuronal workspace theory of consciousness suggests that a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely when that information enters consciousness.(no-match)-
Philosophers and scientists have sought to explain the subjective nature of consciousness and its relation to physical processes in the brain.(no-match)-
The quest to explain consciousness has led to various theories of consciousness evolving in parallel and often providing incompatible accounts of the neural basis of consciousness.(no-match)-
Empirical support for a given theory of consciousness is often highly dependent on methodological choices.(no-match)-
The authors conducted a large-scale, open-science adversarial collaboration to test the integrated information theory and the global neuronal workspace theory.(no-match)-
The researchers said that integrated information theory says consciousness comes from the interaction and cooperation of various parts of the brain as they work together to integrate information.(no-match)-
The researchers said that the study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support the integrated information theory.(no-match)-
The researchers said that the study did not find enough support for the global neuronal workspace theory.(no-match)-
The study involved 256 subjects.(no-match)-
The scientists showed the 256 subjects various visual stimuli.(no-match)-
The scientists used three common human brain measurement tools that track blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity to study the subjects’ brains.(no-match)-
The study showed functional connection between neurons in early visual areas of the brain and frontal areas of the brain.(no-match)-
The findings de-emphasize the importance of the prefrontal cortex in consciousness.(no-match)-
The findings suggest that consciousness itself may be linked with sensory processing and perception.(no-match)-
The findings may shed light on disorders of consciousness such as comas or vegetative states.(no-match)-
✓ Identifying where consciousness comes from could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries.Identifying the neural origin of consciousness could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries, such as those in comas.0.916
Professor Anil Seth of the University of Sussex said that no single experiment would decisively refute either theory.(no-match)-
Professor Anil Seth of the University of Sussex said that the theories are too different in their assumptions and explanatory goals for one theory to conclusively defeat the other.(no-match)-
Professor Anil Seth of the University of Sussex said that the findings of the collaboration are extremely valuable.(no-match)-
The findings appear this week in the journal Nature.(no-match)-
O. Ferrante et al. published the article Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness in Nature on April 30, 2025.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • A multinational adversarial collaboration tested two theories of consciousness: Integrated Information Theory (IIT) and Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT).
  • Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT) posits that a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely when it enters consciousness.
  • The consciousness adversarial-collaboration study involved 256 subjects.
  • Professor Anil Seth is affiliated with the University of Sussex.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study used three common brain measurement tools tracking blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support IIT, nor sufficient support for GNWT in the front of the brain.
  • The IIT vs GNWT adversarial collaboration was published as 'Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness' in Nature on April 30, 2025, with DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.
  • Convergence on a broadly accepted neuroscientific theory of consciousness would have profound medical, societal, and ethical implications.
  • The consciousness study's findings suggest the prefrontal cortex, while important for reasoning and planning, may not be central to consciousness itself, which appears linked to sensory processing and perception.

  • MHC class II deficiency, also known as Bare Lymphocyte Syndrome (BLS), is a rare combined immunodeficiency caused by mutations in genes regulating expression of MHCII molecules.

  • Absence of MHCII expression causes abnormal cross-talk with developing T cells, likely leading to defects in thymic epithelial cells (TEC).

  • Henry David Thoreau described his meditative yoga practice as allowing him to see the world more clearly and more beautifully.

  • Hatha yoga is the practice of postures, breath control, and meditation to strengthen the body and promote union with the Supreme Self.
  • Indra Devi's Hatha yoga focused on asana, or postures, to promote youth, health, and physically fit bodies, and she did not include the religious philosophy of yoga in her teaching.
  • As of July 2012, 15 million Americans were reported to practice yoga, 72.2% female and 27.8% male.
  • The 1937 film 'Lost Horizon' told a story similar to Theos Bernard's adventures in India learning yoga postures and philosophy.

  • 55.9% of patients in the Winship childhood cancer survivorship study received a survivorship care plan (SCP).
  • The Winship survivorship-care-plan study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.
  • A survivorship care plan (SCP) is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history and outlines recommended screenings, late-effect monitoring, and healthy-living guidance.
  • Childhood cancer survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits.
  • Cancer SurvivorLink is a web-based platform developed by Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, to help survivors and clinicians access survivorship care plans.

Qi is the traditional Chinese medicine concept of the body's life force energy.(no-match)-
Imbalances or disruptions of Qi can result from diet, lifestyle, environment, injuries, or excessive emotions.(no-match)-

  • Traditional Chinese Medicine originated over 5,000 years ago and is rooted in the ancient philosophy of Taoism.
  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, Qi (pronounced chee) is described as the body's life force energy.
  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, acupuncture involves insertion of very thin needles through the skin at points positioned along pathways called meridians.
  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, acupuncture regulates and restores the normal flow of Qi, restoring health in the process.
  • Meridians in Traditional Chinese Medicine have been shown in recent research to be real, anatomical structures potentially involved in tissue restoration and repair.
  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, an imbalance or disruption of Qi may result in physical and/or emotional pathologies.
  • Chinese herbal medicine is typically prescribed as formulas rather than individual herbs, tailored to a specific TCM diagnostic pattern.

A microdiscectomy is a surgical procedure used to treat a one-level herniated or slipped disc.(no-match)-
Microdiscectomies are performed by neurosurgeons or orthopedic spine surgeons.(no-match)-

✓ The incision size for a minimally invasive microdiscectomy is no more than 2 centimeters.In a minimally invasive microdiscectomy the incision is no more than 2 centimeters.0.952

CNSO reports a 98% patient satisfaction rate for microdiscectomy procedures.(no-match)-
The recurrent lumbar disc herniation (RLDH) rate for CNSO patients is less than 1%.(no-match)-

Factors that may prolong recovery time include excessive activity, failure to follow medication plans, sedentary behavior, and chronic conditions like diabetes.(no-match)-

  • The goal of a microdiscectomy is to alleviate pressure on the spinal cord or on an aggravated, pinched nerve.
  • At Centers for Neurosurgery, Spine & Orthopedics (CNSO), patients have a 98% satisfaction rate and the recurrent lumbar disc herniation rate is less than 1%.

  • Iqbal Akhtar is a scholar of religion at Florida International University.
  • During Ramadan, Muslims abstain from food, water, smoking, sex, and all sensory pleasures from dawn to dusk.
  • In Islam, fasting develops submission to God, empathy with the poor, and repentance, and provides time for spiritual introspection.
  • According to the 12th-century theologian al-Ghazali, fasting can allow the believer to better perceive the ultimate reality of God as it involves all five senses — touch, sight, hearing, smell, and taste.
  • Jainism is an ancient religion from India that espouses nonviolence, nonpossessiveness, and pluralism.
  • In Jainism, fasting falls under tapas or asceticism, which varies by degree between the laity and monastics.
  • Paryushan is a Jain holiday observed annually around August to September, lasting 8 to 10 days.
  • During Paryushan, Jains focus on values including forgiveness, humility, straightforwardness, truth, contentment, self-restraint, penance, renunciation, nonattachment, and celibacy.
  • Mircea Eliade described sacred time as lying outside of ordinary time, and fasting as one way to enter it.
  • Confucians use the term 'ren' to describe a type of wisdom from fasting that translates as humanity, humaneness, goodness, benevolence, or love.
  • 'The Fast Diet' book advocates eating normally on select days and drastically reducing calories on the remaining days.

Precision medicine uses genomic information to guide medical decision making.(no-match)-
Targeted therapy blocks cancer cell growth by interfering with molecules involved in cancer development.(no-match)-

Overexpression of the Her-2 protein on cell membranes leads to unregulated cell growth and tumor formation.(no-match)-

✓ Pembrolizumab, sold under the brand name Keytruda, is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD-1 receptor on T-lymphocytes.Pembrolizumab (brand name Keytruda) is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD-1 (programmed cell death 1) receptor.0.939
Pembrolizumab inhibits the PD-1 pathway to activate the immune system and promote T cell proliferation.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab is effective against lung, bladder, head and neck, renal cell, and Hodgkin lymphoma cancers.(no-match)-
Former US president Jimmy Carter received Keytruda for metastatic melanoma.(no-match)-
Yervoy (Ipilimumab) was the first checkpoint inhibitor released in 2011.(no-match)-
The monthly cost for an average dose of pembrolizumab is approximately $14,500.(no-match)-

Tumors with a high number of genetic mutations are more susceptible to checkpoint inhibitor activity.(no-match)-

Melanoma and lymphoma show consistent susceptibility to checkpoint inhibitors.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003 and decoded the entire human genome composed of 3 billion DNA base pairs.
  • The human exome contains approximately 23,000 genes and represents about 2% of the total DNA content of the genome.
  • The US Precision Medicine Initiative launched in 2015 placed cancer research and treatment at the forefront of US medical priorities.
  • Checkpoint inhibitors have been shown to be effective against lung cancer, bladder cancer, head and neck cancer, renal cell cancer, and Hodgkin lymphoma.
  • Cancer cells evade immune system surveillance through a process called immunoediting.
  • Tumors stimulate upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion and produce an immune tolerant environment via cytokine production.
  • Cancer avoids immune system surveillance by creating new surface proteins called neoantigens that are difficult to recognize as foreign, or by reducing antigenic proteins on the cell surface.
  • Former US President Jimmy Carter received pembrolizumab (Keytruda) for metastatic melanoma to the brain, and his brain tumor shrunk and became undetectable on imaging after treatment.
src 19 · Seven-Year Experiment Uncovers New Insights into Nature of ... · hits 1/11 · bl 0 · emitted 11
EmittedMatched GTScore
A multinational team of neuroscientists tested the integrated information theory (IIT) and the global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT).(no-match)-
✓ Integrated information theory suggests consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.Integrated Information Theory (IIT) posits that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.0.929
Global neuronal workspace theory suggests a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely to produce conscious experience.(no-match)-
The study involved 256 subjects.(no-match)-
Researchers used three brain measurement tools to track blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity in the brains of subjects.(no-match)-
The study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support the integrated information theory.(no-match)-
The study did not find enough support for the global neuronal workspace theory's claim that consciousness happens in the front of the brain.(no-match)-
The study found functional connections between neurons in early visual areas and frontal areas of the brain.(no-match)-
The findings suggest consciousness may be linked with sensory processing and perception rather than the prefrontal cortex.(no-match)-
The findings of the study were published in the journal Nature on April 30, 2025.(no-match)-
The study was conducted by the Cogitate Consortium, led by O. Ferrante.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • A multinational adversarial collaboration tested two theories of consciousness: Integrated Information Theory (IIT) and Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT).
  • Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT) posits that a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely when it enters consciousness.
  • The consciousness adversarial-collaboration study involved 256 subjects.
  • Professor Anil Seth is affiliated with the University of Sussex.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study used three common brain measurement tools tracking blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support IIT, nor sufficient support for GNWT in the front of the brain.
  • The IIT vs GNWT adversarial collaboration was published as 'Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness' in Nature on April 30, 2025, with DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.
  • Identifying the neural origin of consciousness could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries, such as those in comas.
  • Convergence on a broadly accepted neuroscientific theory of consciousness would have profound medical, societal, and ethical implications.
  • The consciousness study's findings suggest the prefrontal cortex, while important for reasoning and planning, may not be central to consciousness itself, which appears linked to sensory processing and perception.

  • Buddhist philosophy holds that everything is empty of inherent existence.
  • David Bohm's model of reality posits an implicate and explicate order.
  • Alfred North Whitehead's philosophical view describes reality as a grand interwoven process.

✓ MHCII deficiency results in impaired cellular and humoral immune responses, leading to severe infections and autoimmunity.MHCII deficiency results in impaired cellular and humoral immune responses, leading to severe infections and autoimmunity.1.000
✓ Abnormal cross-talk with developing T cells due to the absence of MHCII expression likely leads to defects in thymic epithelial cells (TEC).Absence of MHCII expression causes abnormal cross-talk with developing T cells, likely leading to defects in thymic epithelial cells (TEC).0.945

We have performed an in-depth cellular and molecular characterization of TEC in this disease.(no-match)-

✓ Impairment of lymphostromal cross-talk in the thymus of MHCII−/−mice affects mTEC maturation and promiscuous gene expression and causes defects of central tolerance.In MHCII−/− mice, impaired lymphostromal cross-talk in the thymus affects medullary thymic epithelial cell (mTEC) maturation and promiscuous gene expression and causes defects of central tolerance.0.933
We observed peripheral tolerance impairment, likely due to defective Treg cell generation and/or function and B cell tolerance breakdown.(no-match)-

  • MHC class II deficiency, also known as Bare Lymphocyte Syndrome (BLS), is a rare combined immunodeficiency caused by mutations in genes regulating expression of MHCII molecules.
  • The article 'Thymic Epithelial Cell Alterations and Defective Thymopoiesis Lead to Central and Peripheral Tolerance Perturbation in MHCII Deficiency' was published in Frontiers in Immunology on 2021-06-15.
  • DOI 10.3389/fimmu.2021.669943 identifies the Ferrua et al. study on MHCII deficiency and thymic epithelial cell alterations.
  • Peripheral tolerance impairment in MHCII deficiency is likely due to defective regulatory T cell (Treg) generation and/or function and B cell tolerance breakdown.
  • Disease-specific thymic epithelial cell defects in MHCII deficiency limit the potential benefits of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.

Missed GT:
  • Gregory Shushan authored an article exploring the diversity and similarities of near-death experiences (NDEs) across different cultures and historical contexts.
  • Shushan's article argues for a multifaceted understanding of near-death experiences incorporating universal, cultural, and individual factors.
  • Shushan's article examines the tension between materialist and metaphysical explanations of near-death experiences.
  • Shushan's article discusses the implications of near-death experiences for the survival hypothesis.
  • Shushan's article highlights that near-death experiences are interpreted spiritually despite cultural variations.
src 6 · Winship study links survivorship care to longer life for childhood ... · hits 1/12 · bl 0 · emitted 19
EmittedMatched GTScore
A new study led by researchers at Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta shows that childhood cancer survivors who engage in long-term survivorship programs and receive a personalized survivorship care plan (SCP) live longer than those who do not.(no-match)-
The research was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.(no-match)-
The study provides the first evidence that survivorship care plans, when integrated into comprehensive pediatric survivorship programs, are associated with significantly improved survival.(no-match)-
The multidisciplinary study was co-led by Sharon M. Castellino, MD, MSc, professor and pediatric oncologist at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta and researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.(no-match)-
✓ Lead author Xu Ji, PhD, MSPH, is a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute and associate professor in the Department of Pediatrics at Emory University School of Medicine and the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta.Xu Ji, PhD, MSPH, is a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute and associate professor in the Department of Pediatrics at Emory University School of Medicine.0.906
The research team followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016 who were eligible for a survivorship program at a comprehensive pediatric cancer center.(no-match)-
The survivors were tracked for outcomes for up to 10 years.(no-match)-
Just over half of patients studied (55.9%) received a survivorship care plan (SCP).(no-match)-
Those who received an SCP had a 38% lower risk of death over 10 years compared with those who did not.(no-match)-
Survivors with multiple survivorship visits and SCP updates experienced even greater survival benefits.(no-match)-
SCP recipients were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes than survivors who never received a plan.(no-match)-
Previous studies had not shown a direct link between SCPs and survival.(no-match)-
This research is the first large, contemporary, and diverse cohort study to link SCP receipt within a pediatric cancer survivorship program to improved survival.(no-match)-
Researchers found that SCP engagement was not uniform across demographic or clinical groups.(no-match)-
Survivors who were older, identified as non-Hispanic Black, or had central nervous system tumors or surgery-only treatment were less likely to receive an SCP.(no-match)-
The team plans to continue tracking survivors beyond 10 years, examine quality of life alongside survival, and explore web-based platforms to help survivors and clinicians access SCPs nationwide.(no-match)-
One such platform is Cancer SurvivorLink, developed by coauthor Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, professor and co-director of the Aflac Survivor Program at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta and researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.(no-match)-
The multidisciplinary study team included researchers from Winship Cancer Institute, the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta, and Emory University’s School of Medicine and Rollins School of Public Health.(no-match)-
This work was supported in part by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (Award SIP 20-004; Cooperative Agreement U48DP006377), the National Cancer Institute (R50CA285492) and the National Institute on Minority Health and Health Disparities (K01MD018637).(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • A Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children's Healthcare of Atlanta study found that childhood cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan had a 38% lower risk of death over 10 years compared with those who did not.
  • The survivorship care plan study followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016 for up to 10 years.
  • 55.9% of patients in the Winship childhood cancer survivorship study received a survivorship care plan (SCP).
  • Sharon M. Castellino, MD, MSc, is a professor and pediatric oncologist at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta.
  • The Winship survivorship-care-plan study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.
  • A survivorship care plan (SCP) is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history and outlines recommended screenings, late-effect monitoring, and healthy-living guidance.
  • Childhood cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes than survivors who never received a plan.
  • Childhood cancer survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits.
  • Older childhood cancer survivors, those identifying as non-Hispanic Black, those with central nervous system tumors, and those who received surgery-only treatment were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.
  • Cancer SurvivorLink is a web-based platform developed by Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, to help survivors and clinicians access survivorship care plans.
  • Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, is a professor and co-director of the Aflac Survivor Program at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta and a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.
src 7 · Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies · hits 0/8 · bl 0 · emitted 6
EmittedMatched GTScore
The paper titled 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies: A Systematic Mapping Study' was submitted on 8 Jan 2025 (v1) and last revised on 22 Aug 2025 (v3).(no-match)-
The paper reviews 39 studies that discuss ethical concerns and mitigation strategies related to Large Language Models (LLMs).(no-match)-
The paper analyzes ethical concerns using five ethical dimensions extracted from existing guidelines, frameworks, and an analysis of mitigation strategies and implementation challenges.(no-match)-
The paper's findings reveal that ethical concerns in LLMs are multi-dimensional and context-dependent.(no-match)-
The paper concludes that ethical issues often hinder the practical implementation of mitigation strategies in high-stake areas like healthcare and public governance.(no-match)-
The paper states that existing frameworks often lack adaptability, failing to accommodate evolving societal expectations and diverse contexts.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies: A Systematic Mapping Study' conducted a systematic mapping study reviewing 39 studies on ethical concerns and mitigation strategies related to LLMs.
  • The systematic mapping study on LLM ethics analyzed ethical concerns using five ethical dimensions extracted from existing guidelines and frameworks.
  • The paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI' was first submitted to arXiv on 8 January 2025 as 2502.00015.
  • Chetan Arora is one of the authors of the arXiv paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies'.
  • Ethical concerns in Large Language Models are multi-dimensional and context-dependent.
  • Ethical issues often hinder the practical implementation of LLM mitigation strategies, particularly in high-stake areas like healthcare and public governance.
  • Existing frameworks for LLM ethics often lack adaptability, failing to accommodate evolving societal expectations and diverse contexts.
  • Mitigation strategies for ethical challenges in Large Language Models are complex and domain-dependent.

Advaita Vedanta posits that Brahman and Atman are fundamentally identical.(no-match)-
Brahman is the unchanging, eternal, and formless reality underlying the universe.(no-match)-
Brahman is pure consciousness, existence, and bliss, collectively described as sat-cit-ananda.(no-match)-
Brahman is the substratum of all existence.(no-match)-
Brahman is self-manifesting, self-illuminating, and beyond categorization.(no-match)-
Advaita Vedanta asserts the identity of Atman with Brahman.(no-match)-
The Upanishadic mahavakya 'Tat Tvam Asi' articulates the unity of Atman and Brahman.(no-match)-
The perception of separation between Atman and Brahman is attributed to ignorance (avidya).(no-match)-
Maya is the cosmic illusion that creates the appearance of duality and multiplicity.(no-match)-
Maya veils the true nature of Brahman and projects empirical reality.(no-match)-
Maya makes the unchanging Brahman appear as the dynamic, ever-changing phenomenal world.(no-match)-
Paramarthika is the absolute reality of Brahman, unchanging and beyond time and space.(no-match)-
Vyavaharika is the empirical reality of the everyday world subject to change and duality.(no-match)-
Prabhasika is illusory reality, such as dreams or mirages, which are subjective and transient.(no-match)-
Liberation (Moksha) involves realizing the non-dual nature of Brahman through knowledge (jnana).(no-match)-
Meditation and study of scriptures help transcend ignorance and reveal the unity of Atman and Brahman.(no-match)-
Consciousness (cit) is the essence of Brahman.(no-match)-
Consciousness is self-luminous, eternal, and transcends subject-object distinctions.(no-match)-
The pure consciousness of Brahman is non-relational and undifferentiated.(no-match)-
Sakshin refers to the witnessing consciousness within the individual.(no-match)-
Sakshin remains untouched by the mind-body complex.(no-match)-
Sakshin illuminates the empirical self (jiva) and its experiences.(no-match)-
The jiva is a reflection of Brahman, distorted by limiting adjuncts (upadhis) of the body, mind, and senses.(no-match)-
Self-inquiry and meditation help the jiva transcend limitations and recognize its identity with Brahman.(no-match)-
Maya creates the illusion of duality, obscuring the unity of Atman and Brahman.(no-match)-
Maya's influence attributes the experience of individual agency, subject-object relationships, and multiplicity.(no-match)-
Liberation involves overcoming Maya through knowledge, meditation, and direct experiential realization (samadhi).(no-match)-
In the state of realization, the distinction between knower, knowing, and known dissolves.(no-match)-
Advaita Vedanta provides a profound understanding of existence, consciousness, and the nature of reality.(no-match)-
Advaita Vedanta has implications for modern psychology in exploring non-dual awareness and higher states of consciousness.(no-match)-
Advaita Vedanta has influenced spiritual movements and practices worldwide.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • In Advaita Vedanta, the term 'Advaita' translates to 'non-duality', emphasizing the indivisible unity of all existence.
  • Advaita Vedanta holds that Brahman (ultimate reality) and Atman (individual self) are fundamentally identical.
  • Brahman in Advaita Vedanta is described as sat-cit-ananda — pure existence, consciousness, and bliss.
  • The Upanishadic mahavakya 'Tat Tvam Asi' ('Thou art That') articulates the identity of Atman and Brahman in Advaita Vedanta.
  • Avidya (ignorance) in Advaita Vedanta is said to veil the true nature of the self and cause the perception of separation between Atman and Brahman.
  • Maya in Advaita Vedanta is the cosmic illusion that creates the appearance of duality and multiplicity in the world.
  • In Advaita Vedanta, through Maya, the unchanging Brahman appears as the dynamic, ever-changing phenomenal world.
  • Advaita Vedanta distinguishes three levels of reality: Paramarthika (absolute), Vyavaharika (empirical), and Prabhasika (illusory).
  • Moksha in Advaita Vedanta is liberation attained by realizing the non-dual nature of Brahman through jnana (knowledge).
  • In Advaita Vedanta, liberation is aided through practices such as meditation and study of scriptures, which help transcend ignorance.
  • Sakshin in Advaita Vedanta is the witnessing consciousness that remains untouched by the mind-body complex.

✓ In 2015, the US Precision Medicine Initiative placed cancer research and treatment at the forefront of medical priorities in the country.The US Precision Medicine Initiative launched in 2015 placed cancer research and treatment at the forefront of US medical priorities.0.959
Precision medicine incorporates the use of genomic information to guide medical decision making.(no-match)-
Targeted therapy is a type of cancer treatment that blocks cancer cell growth by interfering with targeted molecules involved with development of cancer.(no-match)-
✓ Herceptin is a targeted therapy used in early and late stage breast cancer treatment.Herceptin is a targeted therapy used for early and late stage breast cancer that acts on the human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her-2).0.918
Herceptin acts on the receptor site for human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her-2).(no-match)-
The Her-2 pathway promotes growth and cell division when it is functioning normally.(no-match)-
When the Her-2 protein is overexpressed on cell membranes there is unregulated cell growth and tumor formation.(no-match)-
✓ Herceptin blocks the Her-2 receptor function and prevents cancer cell proliferation.Herceptin blocks the Her-2 receptor function and prevents cancer cell proliferation.1.000
Targeted breast cancer drugs may work when conventional chemotherapy does not work.(no-match)-
Targeted breast cancer drugs may work in concert with other treatments.(no-match)-

Immunotherapy uses the body’s immune system to fight cancer.(no-match)-
The immune system is efficient at identifying and destroying tumors even after early malignant transformation.(no-match)-
Malignant clones are able to evade the immune system’s surveillance through a complex process called immunoediting.(no-match)-
✓ Cancer avoids the immune system’s surveillance by reducing antigenic proteins on the cell surface or creating new surface proteins called neoantigens.Cancer avoids immune system surveillance by creating new surface proteins called neoantigens that are difficult to recognize as foreign, or by reducing antigenic proteins on the cell surface.0.956
Tumors stimulate upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion.(no-match)-
Tumors produce an immune tolerant environment via cytokine production.(no-match)-
Specific cytokines are able to suppress immune responses and promote growth of the tumor.(no-match)-
Checkpoint inhibitors are drugs that are monoclonal antibodies.(no-match)-
Checkpoint inhibitors bind check point receptor sites on T lymphocytes.(no-match)-
✓ Pembrolizumab, brand name Keytruda, is a monoclonal antibody that binds to PD-1 receptor on T-lymphocyte.Pembrolizumab (brand name Keytruda) is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD-1 (programmed cell death 1) receptor.0.957
Pembrolizumab inhibits the PD-1 pathway that depresses the responsiveness of the immune system.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab results in activation of the immune system with T cell proliferation, increased immune mediators, and tumor destruction.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab has been shown to be effective against lung, bladder, head and neck, renal cell, and Hodgkin lymphoma cancers.(no-match)-
✓ Former US president Jimmy Carter is receiving Keytruda (pembrolizumab) for metastatic melanoma to the brain.Former US President Jimmy Carter received pembrolizumab (Keytruda) for metastatic melanoma to the brain, and his brain tumor shrunk and became undetectable on imaging after treatment.0.914
Jimmy Carter’s brain tumor has shrunk and is no longer detectable on imaging studies after use of pembrolizumab.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab is more tolerable than the first checkpoint inhibitor Yervoy (Ipilimumab) that was released 2011.(no-match)-
The monthly cost for pembrolizumab at the average dose is around $14,500.(no-match)-
Higher doses of pembrolizumab used in clinical trials cost up to $83,000.(no-match)-
Immunotherapy does not work for all people with the same cancers nor does it work for all types of cancers.(no-match)-
Tumors with a high number of genetic mutations seem to be susceptible to checkpoint inhibitor activity.(no-match)-
✓ Cancers that are hypermutated result from carcinogen exposure like tobacco and ultraviolet light.Cancers that are hypermutated can result from exposure to carcinogens such as tobacco and ultraviolet light.0.976
Melanoma and lymphoma generate a robust immune response and have shown consistent susceptibility to the action of checkpoint inhibitors.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The human exome contains approximately 23,000 genes and represents about 2% of the total DNA content of the genome.
  • Checkpoint inhibitors are monoclonal antibody drugs that bind checkpoint receptor sites on T lymphocytes to allow the immune system to kill tumor cells.
  • Checkpoint inhibitors have been shown to be effective against lung cancer, bladder cancer, head and neck cancer, renal cell cancer, and Hodgkin lymphoma.
  • Cancer cells evade immune system surveillance through a process called immunoediting.
  • Tumors stimulate upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion and produce an immune tolerant environment via cytokine production.
src 19 · Seven-Year Experiment Uncovers New Insights into Nature of ... · hits 3/11 · bl 0 · emitted 16
EmittedMatched GTScore
✓ A multinational team of neuroscientists tested two competing theories of consciousness: the integrated information theory (IIT) and the global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT).A multinational adversarial collaboration tested two theories of consciousness: Integrated Information Theory (IIT) and Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT).0.935
✓ Integrated information theory suggests that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.Integrated Information Theory (IIT) posits that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.0.933
Global neuronal workspace theory suggests a network of brain areas will spotlight important pieces of information in the brain and broadcast it widely the moment it enters consciousness.(no-match)-
Philosophers and scientists have sought to explain the subjective nature of consciousness and how it relates to physical processes in the brain.(no-match)-
Empirical support for a given theory of consciousness is often highly dependent on methodological choices.(no-match)-
The authors tested IIT and GNWT theories through a large-scale, open-science adversarial collaboration.(no-match)-
The study involved 256 subjects, which is unprecedented for this kind of experiment.(no-match)-
Scientists showed subjects various visual stimuli and used three common human brain measurement tools to study their brains.(no-match)-
The study showed functional connection between neurons in early visual areas of the brain and the frontal areas of the brain.(no-match)-
The findings de-emphasize the importance of the prefrontal cortex in consciousness.(no-match)-
The findings suggest consciousness may be linked with sensory processing and perception.(no-match)-
The findings have implications for understanding disorders of consciousness such as comas or vegetative states.(no-match)-
✓ Identifying where consciousness comes from could help detect ‘covert consciousness’ in unresponsive patients with severe injuries.Identifying the neural origin of consciousness could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries, such as those in comas.0.904
Professor Anil Seth said no single experiment would decisively refute either theory.(no-match)-
The findings appear in the journal Nature.(no-match)-
O. Ferrante et al. (Cogitate Consortium) published the study in Nature on April 30, 2025.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT) posits that a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely when it enters consciousness.
  • The consciousness adversarial-collaboration study involved 256 subjects.
  • Professor Anil Seth is affiliated with the University of Sussex.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study used three common brain measurement tools tracking blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support IIT, nor sufficient support for GNWT in the front of the brain.
  • The IIT vs GNWT adversarial collaboration was published as 'Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness' in Nature on April 30, 2025, with DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.
  • Convergence on a broadly accepted neuroscientific theory of consciousness would have profound medical, societal, and ethical implications.
  • The consciousness study's findings suggest the prefrontal cortex, while important for reasoning and planning, may not be central to consciousness itself, which appears linked to sensory processing and perception.

Major Histocompatibility Complex class II deficiency leads to severe infections and autoimmunity.(no-match)-

Missed GT:
  • MHC class II deficiency, also known as Bare Lymphocyte Syndrome (BLS), is a rare combined immunodeficiency caused by mutations in genes regulating expression of MHCII molecules.
  • MHCII deficiency results in impaired cellular and humoral immune responses, leading to severe infections and autoimmunity.
  • In MHCII−/− mice, impaired lymphostromal cross-talk in the thymus affects medullary thymic epithelial cell (mTEC) maturation and promiscuous gene expression and causes defects of central tolerance.
  • The article 'Thymic Epithelial Cell Alterations and Defective Thymopoiesis Lead to Central and Peripheral Tolerance Perturbation in MHCII Deficiency' was published in Frontiers in Immunology on 2021-06-15.
  • DOI 10.3389/fimmu.2021.669943 identifies the Ferrua et al. study on MHCII deficiency and thymic epithelial cell alterations.

src 6 · Winship study links survivorship care to longer life for childhood ... · hits 3/12 · bl 0 · emitted 15
EmittedMatched GTScore
A study led by researchers at Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta found that childhood cancer survivors who receive a personalized survivorship care plan (SCP) live longer than those who do not.(no-match)-
The study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.(no-match)-
✓ Sharon M. Castellino, MD, MSc, is a professor and pediatric oncologist at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center and a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.Sharon M. Castellino, MD, MSc, is a professor and pediatric oncologist at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta.0.967
Xu Ji, PhD, MSPH, is the lead author of the study and an associate professor in the Department of Pediatrics at Emory University School of Medicine.(no-match)-
The research team followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016.(no-match)-
The study tracked patient outcomes for up to 10 years.(no-match)-
✓ A survivorship care plan (SCP) is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history and outlines recommended screenings and late-effect monitoring.A survivorship care plan (SCP) is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history and outlines recommended screenings, late-effect monitoring, and healthy-living guidance.0.993
55.9% of the patients in the study received a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
Childhood cancer survivors who received an SCP had a 38% lower risk of death over 10 years compared to those who did not receive one.(no-match)-
Survivors with multiple survivorship visits and SCP updates experienced greater survival benefits than those with fewer visits.(no-match)-
SCP recipients were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes than survivors who never received a plan.(no-match)-
Survivors who were older or identified as non-Hispanic Black were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
Survivors with central nervous system tumors or surgery-only treatment were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
✓ Cancer SurvivorLink is a web-based platform developed by Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, to help survivors and clinicians access survivorship care plans.Cancer SurvivorLink is a web-based platform developed by Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, to help survivors and clinicians access survivorship care plans.1.000
The study was supported in part by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, the National Cancer Institute, and the National Institute on Minority Health and Health Disparities.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • A Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children's Healthcare of Atlanta study found that childhood cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan had a 38% lower risk of death over 10 years compared with those who did not.
  • The survivorship care plan study followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016 for up to 10 years.
  • 55.9% of patients in the Winship childhood cancer survivorship study received a survivorship care plan (SCP).
  • Xu Ji, PhD, MSPH, is a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute and associate professor in the Department of Pediatrics at Emory University School of Medicine.
  • The Winship survivorship-care-plan study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.
  • Childhood cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes than survivors who never received a plan.
  • Childhood cancer survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits.
  • Older childhood cancer survivors, those identifying as non-Hispanic Black, those with central nervous system tumors, and those who received surgery-only treatment were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.
  • Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, is a professor and co-director of the Aflac Survivor Program at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta and a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.
src 7 · Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies · hits 1/8 · bl 0 · emitted 10
EmittedMatched GTScore
Generative AI technologies, particularly Large Language Models (LLMs), enhance efficiency in information retrieval, content generation, and decision-making processes.(no-match)-
The deployment of Large Language Models presents diverse ethical challenges.(no-match)-
A systematic mapping study reviewed 39 studies discussing ethical concerns and mitigation strategies related to Large Language Models.(no-match)-
Ethical concerns in Large Language Models are analyzed using five ethical dimensions extracted from existing guidelines and frameworks.(no-match)-
✓ Ethical concerns associated with Large Language Models are multi-dimensional and context-dependent.Ethical concerns in Large Language Models are multi-dimensional and context-dependent.0.988
Ethical issues often hinder the practical implementation of mitigation strategies in high-stake areas such as healthcare and public governance.(no-match)-
Existing ethical frameworks for Large Language Models often lack adaptability to evolving societal expectations and diverse contexts.(no-match)-
The paper titled 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies: A Systematic Mapping Study' was submitted to arXiv on January 8, 2025.(no-match)-

Chetan Arora is the individual who submitted the study to the arXiv repository.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies: A Systematic Mapping Study' conducted a systematic mapping study reviewing 39 studies on ethical concerns and mitigation strategies related to LLMs.
  • The systematic mapping study on LLM ethics analyzed ethical concerns using five ethical dimensions extracted from existing guidelines and frameworks.
  • The paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI' was first submitted to arXiv on 8 January 2025 as 2502.00015.
  • Chetan Arora is one of the authors of the arXiv paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies'.
  • Ethical issues often hinder the practical implementation of LLM mitigation strategies, particularly in high-stake areas like healthcare and public governance.
  • Existing frameworks for LLM ethics often lack adaptability, failing to accommodate evolving societal expectations and diverse contexts.
  • Mitigation strategies for ethical challenges in Large Language Models are complex and domain-dependent.

✓ Adi Shankaracharya extensively formulated the school of Advaita Vedanta.Adi Shankaracharya formulated Advaita Vedanta extensively.0.936

Brahman is described as the unchanging, eternal, and formless reality underlying the universe.(no-match)-
Brahman is characterized as sat-cit-ananda, which translates to pure consciousness, existence, and bliss.(no-match)-
✓ The Upanishadic mahavakya 'Tat Tvam Asi' translates to 'Thou art That' and articulates the identity of Atman with Brahman.The Upanishadic mahavakya 'Tat Tvam Asi' ('Thou art That') articulates the identity of Atman and Brahman in Advaita Vedanta.0.935
Avidya, or ignorance, is the cause of the perception of separation between Atman and Brahman.(no-match)-
Maya is the cosmic illusion that creates the appearance of duality and multiplicity in the world.(no-match)-
Advaita Vedanta defines Paramarthika as the absolute reality of Brahman, which is beyond time and space.(no-match)-
Advaita Vedanta defines Vyavaharika as the empirical reality of the everyday world of perception.(no-match)-
Advaita Vedanta defines Prabhasika as illusory reality, such as dreams or mirages.(no-match)-
Moksha, or liberation, involves realizing the non-dual nature of Brahman through knowledge (jnana).(no-match)-
✓ Sakshin refers to the witnessing consciousness within an individual that remains untouched by the mind-body complex.Sakshin in Advaita Vedanta is the witnessing consciousness that remains untouched by the mind-body complex.0.916
The jiva, or empirical self, is considered a reflection of Brahman distorted by the body, mind, and senses.(no-match)-
Upadhis are the limiting adjuncts of the body, mind, and senses that distort the jiva's perception of Brahman.(no-match)-
Liberation in Advaita Vedanta involves overcoming Maya through knowledge, meditation, and direct experiential realization called samadhi.(no-match)-
In the state of realization, the distinction between the knower, the act of knowing, and the known dissolves.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • Brahman in Advaita Vedanta is described as sat-cit-ananda — pure existence, consciousness, and bliss.
  • Avidya (ignorance) in Advaita Vedanta is said to veil the true nature of the self and cause the perception of separation between Atman and Brahman.
  • Maya in Advaita Vedanta is the cosmic illusion that creates the appearance of duality and multiplicity in the world.
  • In Advaita Vedanta, through Maya, the unchanging Brahman appears as the dynamic, ever-changing phenomenal world.
  • Advaita Vedanta distinguishes three levels of reality: Paramarthika (absolute), Vyavaharika (empirical), and Prabhasika (illusory).
  • Moksha in Advaita Vedanta is liberation attained by realizing the non-dual nature of Brahman through jnana (knowledge).
  • In Advaita Vedanta, liberation is aided through practices such as meditation and study of scriptures, which help transcend ignorance.

  • Meridians in Traditional Chinese Medicine have been shown in recent research to be real, anatomical structures potentially involved in tissue restoration and repair.

src 15 · Recovering from Microdiscectomy · hits 8/10 · bl 0 · emitted 19

✓ A microdiscectomy is a surgical procedure involving the partial or complete removal of a damaged or distorted spinal disc to alleviate pressure on the spinal cord or nerves.A microdiscectomy involves partial to complete removal of a damaged or distorted spinal disc that is causing discomfort or loss of function.0.919
Microdiscectomies are commonly performed by neurosurgeons or orthopedic spine surgeons.(no-match)-
✓ A minimally invasive microdiscectomy for a one-level herniated disc typically involves an incision no larger than 2 centimeters.In a minimally invasive microdiscectomy the incision is no more than 2 centimeters.0.903
Spondylosis or arthritis of the spine may require a larger surgical opening than a standard microdiscectomy to provide a greater field of visualization.(no-match)-
✓ Most patients who undergo a microdiscectomy are able to return home the same day as the procedure.Most patients can go home the same day as a microdiscectomy procedure.0.951
Patients with severe comorbidities may require a short hospital stay for observation following a microdiscectomy.(no-match)-
✓ Most patients experience immediate relief of symptoms after a microdiscectomy.Most patients have immediate relief of symptoms after a microdiscectomy.0.990
✓ Patients can typically resume all activities within 6 weeks after a microdiscectomy, though individual modifications may apply.Within 6 weeks of microdiscectomy, a patient can typically resume all their activities.0.919
✓ Most patients are able to return to non-labor-intensive work within two weeks after a microdiscectomy.Most patients can return to work within two weeks after microdiscectomy, assuming the job is not labor intensive.0.940
Medical providers commonly recommend that patients limit bending, lifting, or twisting movements during the recovery period to prevent disc re-herniation.(no-match)-
Patients are often advised to wait at least two weeks after a microdiscectomy before driving.(no-match)-
Patients involved in strenuous labor or contact sports may need to wait approximately 12 weeks before returning to those activities.(no-match)-
✓ Studies in the National Institutes of Health Library of Medicine indicate that approximately 80% of lumbar discectomies achieve satisfactory outcomes.Approximately 80% of lumbar discectomies achieve satisfactory outcomes according to NIH Library of Medicine studies.0.961
Recurrent lumbar disc herniation incidences are reported in studies to be in the 5% to 18% range.(no-match)-
Centers for Neurosurgery, Spine & Orthopedics (CNSO) reports a 98% patient satisfaction rate for microdiscectomies.(no-match)-
Centers for Neurosurgery, Spine & Orthopedics (CNSO) reports a recurrent lumbar disc herniation rate of less than 1%.(no-match)-
Factors that can prolong recovery time after a microdiscectomy include excessive activity, not following a medication plan, being sedentary, and chronic conditions like diabetes.(no-match)-
Potential risks of microdiscectomy surgery include damage to surrounding tissue, spinal fluid leakage, and adverse reactions to anesthesia.(no-match)-
