Targeted therapy is a cancer treatment that blocks cancer cell growth by interfering with molecules involved in cancer development.(no-match)-
✓ Herceptin is a targeted therapy used in early and late stage breast cancer treatment.Herceptin is a targeted therapy used for early and late stage breast cancer that acts on the human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her-2).0.918
Herceptin acts on the receptor site for human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her-2).(no-match)-
The Her-2 pathway promotes growth and cell division when functioning normally.(no-match)-
Overexpression of the Her-2 protein on cell membranes leads to unregulated cell growth and tumor formation.(no-match)-
✓ Her-2 chemotherapy medications may cause cardiotoxicity, especially if used with anthracycline chemotherapy agents.Her-2 chemotherapy medications may cause cardiotoxicity, especially when used with anthracycline chemotherapy agents.0.994
Immunotherapy uses the body’s immune system to fight cancer.(no-match)-
Immunoediting is a process through which malignant clones evade the immune system’s surveillance.(no-match)-
Tumors stimulate the upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion.(no-match)-
✓ Checkpoint inhibitors are monoclonal antibody drugs that bind to receptor sites on T lymphocytes to allow the immune system to kill tumor cells.Checkpoint inhibitors are monoclonal antibody drugs that bind checkpoint receptor sites on T lymphocytes to allow the immune system to kill tumor cells.0.983
✓ Pembrolizumab, also known by the brand name Keytruda, is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD-1 (programmed cell death 1) receptor on T-lymphocytes.Pembrolizumab (brand name Keytruda) is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD-1 (programmed cell death 1) receptor.0.964
The PD-1 pathway depresses the responsiveness of the immune system.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab has shown effectiveness against lung, bladder, head and neck, renal cell, and Hodgkin lymphoma cancers.(no-match)-
✓ Former US president Jimmy Carter received pembrolizumab for metastatic melanoma to the brain.Former US President Jimmy Carter received pembrolizumab (Keytruda) for metastatic melanoma to the brain, and his brain tumor shrunk and became undetectable on imaging after treatment.0.913
Ipilimumab, brand name Yervoy, was the first checkpoint inhibitor released in 2011.(no-match)-
The monthly cost for pembrolizumab at an average dose is approximately $14,500.(no-match)-
Higher doses of pembrolizumab used in clinical trials can cost up to $83,000.(no-match)-
Tumors with a high number of genetic mutations are generally more susceptible to checkpoint inhibitor activity.(no-match)-
✓ Cancers that are hypermutated can result from exposure to carcinogens such as tobacco and ultraviolet light.Cancers that are hypermutated can result from exposure to carcinogens such as tobacco and ultraviolet light.1.000
Melanoma and lymphoma generate a robust immune response and show consistent susceptibility to checkpoint inhibitors.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003 and decoded the entire human genome composed of 3 billion DNA base pairs.
  • The human exome contains approximately 23,000 genes and represents about 2% of the total DNA content of the genome.
  • The US Precision Medicine Initiative launched in 2015 placed cancer research and treatment at the forefront of US medical priorities.
  • Herceptin blocks the Her-2 receptor function and prevents cancer cell proliferation.
  • Checkpoint inhibitors have been shown to be effective against lung cancer, bladder cancer, head and neck cancer, renal cell cancer, and Hodgkin lymphoma.
  • Cancer cells evade immune system surveillance through a process called immunoediting.
  • Tumors stimulate upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion and produce an immune tolerant environment via cytokine production.
  • Cancer avoids immune system surveillance by creating new surface proteins called neoantigens that are difficult to recognize as foreign, or by reducing antigenic proteins on the cell surface.
src 19 · Seven-Year Experiment Uncovers New Insights into Nature of ... · hits 4/11 · bl 0 · emitted 14
EmittedMatched GTScore
✓ A multinational team of neuroscientists tested the integrated information theory (IIT) and the global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) of consciousness.A multinational adversarial collaboration tested two theories of consciousness: Integrated Information Theory (IIT) and Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT).0.912
✓ Integrated information theory (IIT) suggests that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified as a single whole.Integrated Information Theory (IIT) posits that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.0.976
✓ Global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) suggests that a network of brain areas spotlights and broadcasts important information to produce conscious experience.Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT) posits that a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely when it enters consciousness.0.934
The study of integrated information theory (IIT) and global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) was conducted through a large-scale, open-science adversarial collaboration.(no-match)-
The study involved 256 subjects, which is an unprecedented number for this type of consciousness experiment.(no-match)-
Researchers used three brain measurement tools to track blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity in the subjects.(no-match)-
The study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support the integrated information theory (IIT).(no-match)-
The study did not find enough support for the idea that consciousness happens primarily in the front of the brain as suggested by global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT).(no-match)-
The study showed functional connections between neurons in early visual areas of the brain and the frontal areas of the brain.(no-match)-
The findings de-emphasize the importance of the prefrontal cortex in consciousness, suggesting it is more important for reasoning and planning.(no-match)-
The study suggests that consciousness may be linked with sensory processing and perception rather than intelligence or reasoning.(no-match)-
Professor Anil Seth of the University of Sussex stated that no single experiment would decisively refute either integrated information theory (IIT) or global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT).(no-match)-
The findings of the Cogitate Consortium were published in the journal Nature on April 30, 2025.(no-match)-
✓ Identifying the neural basis of consciousness could help detect 'covert consciousness' in unresponsive patients with severe injuries, such as those in comas or vegetative states.Identifying the neural origin of consciousness could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries, such as those in comas.0.918
Missed GT:
  • The consciousness adversarial-collaboration study involved 256 subjects.
  • Professor Anil Seth is affiliated with the University of Sussex.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study used three common brain measurement tools tracking blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support IIT, nor sufficient support for GNWT in the front of the brain.
  • The IIT vs GNWT adversarial collaboration was published as 'Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness' in Nature on April 30, 2025, with DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.
  • Convergence on a broadly accepted neuroscientific theory of consciousness would have profound medical, societal, and ethical implications.
  • The consciousness study's findings suggest the prefrontal cortex, while important for reasoning and planning, may not be central to consciousness itself, which appears linked to sensory processing and perception.

  • Buddhist philosophy holds that everything is empty of inherent existence.
  • David Bohm's model of reality posits an implicate and explicate order.
  • Alfred North Whitehead's philosophical view describes reality as a grand interwoven process.

A study led by researchers at Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta found that childhood cancer survivors who engage in long-term survivorship programs and receive a personalized survivorship care plan live longer than those who do not.(no-match)-
The childhood cancer survivorship research was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.(no-match)-
The childhood cancer survivorship research provides the first evidence that survivorship care plans integrated into comprehensive pediatric survivorship programs are associated with significantly improved survival.(no-match)-
The multidisciplinary childhood cancer survivorship research was co-led by Sharon M. Castellino, MD, MSc.(no-match)-

The research team followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016.(no-match)-
The 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors were eligible for a survivorship program at a comprehensive pediatric cancer center.(no-match)-

55.9% of the studied patients received a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
A survivorship care plan is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history.(no-match)-
A survivorship care plan outlines recommended screenings, late-effect monitoring, and healthy-living guidance.(no-match)-

Survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits than those with fewer visits or updates.(no-match)-
Recipients of a survivorship care plan were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes compared to survivors who never received a plan.(no-match)-

  • A Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children's Healthcare of Atlanta study found that childhood cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan had a 38% lower risk of death over 10 years compared with those who did not.
  • The survivorship care plan study followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016 for up to 10 years.
  • 55.9% of patients in the Winship childhood cancer survivorship study received a survivorship care plan (SCP).
  • Xu Ji, PhD, MSPH, is a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute and associate professor in the Department of Pediatrics at Emory University School of Medicine.
  • The Winship survivorship-care-plan study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.
  • A survivorship care plan (SCP) is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history and outlines recommended screenings, late-effect monitoring, and healthy-living guidance.
  • Childhood cancer survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits.
  • Cancer SurvivorLink is a web-based platform developed by Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, to help survivors and clinicians access survivorship care plans.

Missed GT:
  • In Advaita Vedanta, the term 'Advaita' translates to 'non-duality', emphasizing the indivisible unity of all existence.
  • Brahman in Advaita Vedanta is described as sat-cit-ananda — pure existence, consciousness, and bliss.
  • Avidya (ignorance) in Advaita Vedanta is said to veil the true nature of the self and cause the perception of separation between Atman and Brahman.
  • Maya in Advaita Vedanta is the cosmic illusion that creates the appearance of duality and multiplicity in the world.
  • In Advaita Vedanta, through Maya, the unchanging Brahman appears as the dynamic, ever-changing phenomenal world.
  • Advaita Vedanta distinguishes three levels of reality: Paramarthika (absolute), Vyavaharika (empirical), and Prabhasika (illusory).
  • Moksha in Advaita Vedanta is liberation attained by realizing the non-dual nature of Brahman through jnana (knowledge).
  • In Advaita Vedanta, liberation is aided through practices such as meditation and study of scriptures, which help transcend ignorance.
  • Sakshin in Advaita Vedanta is the witnessing consciousness that remains untouched by the mind-body complex.

  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, acupuncture involves insertion of very thin needles through the skin at points positioned along pathways called meridians.

Binding checkpoint receptor sites on T lymphocytes allows the immune system to process and kill tumor cells.(no-match)-

Pembrolizumab binds to the PD-1 (programmed cell death 1) receptor on T-lymphocytes.(no-match)-

Pembrolizumab activates the immune system, resulting in T cell proliferation, increased immune mediators, and tumor destruction.(no-match)-

  • Cancer cells evade immune system surveillance through a process called immunoediting.

src 19 · Seven-Year Experiment Uncovers New Insights into Nature of ... · hits 4/11 · bl 0 · emitted 19
EmittedMatched GTScore
A multinational team of neuroscientists tested the integrated information theory (IIT) and the global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) through a large-scale, open-science adversarial collaboration aimed at accelerating progress in consciousness research.(no-match)-
✓ The integrated information theory (IIT) proposes that consciousness emerges when information within a system is highly connected and unified while being consciously perceived.Integrated Information Theory (IIT) posits that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.0.946
✓ The global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) proposes that a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely upon entering consciousness to produce conscious experience.Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT) posits that a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely when it enters consciousness.0.946
Philosophers and scientists have sought to explain the subjective nature of consciousness and its relationship to physical brain processes.(no-match)-
Various theories of consciousness have evolved in parallel and often provide incompatible accounts of the neural basis of consciousness.(no-match)-
Empirical support for theories of consciousness is often highly dependent on methodological choices, indicating confirmation bias in theory testing.(no-match)-
✓ Convergence on a broadly accepted neuroscientific theory of consciousness will have profound medical, societal, and ethical implications.Convergence on a broadly accepted neuroscientific theory of consciousness would have profound medical, societal, and ethical implications.0.987
The integrated information theory (IIT) posits that consciousness arises from the interaction and information sharing among interconnected brain parts rather than from any single brain area.(no-match)-
A seven-year experiment did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support the integrated information theory (IIT).(no-match)-
The seven-year experiment did not find sufficient evidence to support the global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) claim that consciousness occurs in the front of the brain.(no-match)-
The consciousness experiment involved 256 human subjects.(no-match)-
The scientists presented the 256 subjects with visual stimuli and used three brain measurement tools tracking blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity to study the subjects' brains.(no-match)-
The experiment demonstrated a functional connection between neurons in the early visual areas of the brain and the frontal areas of the brain.(no-match)-
The experiment's findings de-emphasize the importance of the prefrontal cortex in consciousness, suggesting consciousness may be linked to sensory processing and perception.(no-match)-
The experiment's findings may shed light on disorders of consciousness such as comas or vegetative states.(no-match)-
✓ Identifying the neural origins of consciousness could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries.Identifying the neural origin of consciousness could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries, such as those in comas.0.977
University of Sussex Professor Anil Seth stated that no single experiment would decisively refute either IIT or GNWT due to their differing assumptions and the coarseness of available experimental methods.(no-match)-
The research paper titled 'Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness' was published in the journal Nature on April 30, 2025.(no-match)-
The research was conducted by O. Ferrante and the Cogitate Consortium.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • A multinational adversarial collaboration tested two theories of consciousness: Integrated Information Theory (IIT) and Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT).
  • The consciousness adversarial-collaboration study involved 256 subjects.
  • Professor Anil Seth is affiliated with the University of Sussex.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study used three common brain measurement tools tracking blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support IIT, nor sufficient support for GNWT in the front of the brain.
  • The IIT vs GNWT adversarial collaboration was published as 'Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness' in Nature on April 30, 2025, with DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.
  • The consciousness study's findings suggest the prefrontal cortex, while important for reasoning and planning, may not be central to consciousness itself, which appears linked to sensory processing and perception.

  • Buddhist philosophy holds that everything is empty of inherent existence.
  • David Bohm's model of reality posits an implicate and explicate order.
  • Alfred North Whitehead's philosophical view describes reality as a grand interwoven process.

  • Swami Vivekananda taught Raja yoga, which focuses on meditation to unite the individual Self with the supreme Self, and pranayama — breathing exercises.
  • Hatha yoga is the practice of postures, breath control, and meditation to strengthen the body and promote union with the Supreme Self.
  • Indra Devi's Hatha yoga focused on asana, or postures, to promote youth, health, and physically fit bodies, and she did not include the religious philosophy of yoga in her teaching.

src 6 · Winship study links survivorship care to longer life for childhood ... · hits 0/12 · bl 0 · emitted 12
EmittedMatched GTScore
A new study led by researchers at Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta found that childhood cancer survivors who engage in long-term survivorship programs and receive a personalized survivorship care plan (SCP) live longer than those who do not.(no-match)-
The research was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute and provides the first evidence that survivorship care plans integrated into comprehensive pediatric survivorship programs are associated with significantly improved survival.(no-match)-
Sharon M. Castellino, MD, MSc, a professor and pediatric oncologist at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta and a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute, co‑led the multidisciplinary study.(no-match)-
Xu Ji, PhD, MSPH, a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute and associate professor in the Department of Pediatrics at Emory University School of Medicine, was the lead author of the study.(no-match)-
The study followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016 who were eligible for a survivorship program at a comprehensive pediatric cancer center, tracking outcomes for up to 10 years.(no-match)-
Among the participants, 55.9% received a survivorship care plan, a personalized document summarizing diagnosis, treatment history, recommended screenings, late‑effect monitoring, and healthy‑living guidance.(no-match)-
Survivors who received an SCP had a 38% lower risk of death over 10 years compared with survivors who did not receive an SCP.(no-match)-
Survivors with multiple survivorship visits and SCP updates experienced greater survival benefits than those with fewer visits or no updates.(no-match)-
When cause of death was examined, SCP recipients were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes than survivors who never received a plan.(no-match)-
The study identified disparities in SCP receipt, noting that older survivors, non‑Hispanic Black survivors, and those with central nervous system tumors or surgery‑only treatment were less likely to receive an SCP.(no-match)-
The research team plans to continue tracking survivors beyond 10 years, examine quality of life alongside survival, and explore web‑based platforms such as Cancer SurvivorLink to help survivors and clinicians access SCPs nationwide.(no-match)-
The study was supported in part by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (Award SIP 20-004; Cooperative Agreement U48DP006377), the National Cancer Institute (R50CA285492), and the National Institute on Minority Health and Health Disparities (K01MD018637).(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • A Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children's Healthcare of Atlanta study found that childhood cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan had a 38% lower risk of death over 10 years compared with those who did not.
  • The survivorship care plan study followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016 for up to 10 years.
  • 55.9% of patients in the Winship childhood cancer survivorship study received a survivorship care plan (SCP).
  • Xu Ji, PhD, MSPH, is a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute and associate professor in the Department of Pediatrics at Emory University School of Medicine.
  • Sharon M. Castellino, MD, MSc, is a professor and pediatric oncologist at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta.
  • The Winship survivorship-care-plan study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.
  • A survivorship care plan (SCP) is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history and outlines recommended screenings, late-effect monitoring, and healthy-living guidance.
  • Childhood cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes than survivors who never received a plan.
  • Childhood cancer survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits.
  • Older childhood cancer survivors, those identifying as non-Hispanic Black, those with central nervous system tumors, and those who received surgery-only treatment were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.
  • Cancer SurvivorLink is a web-based platform developed by Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, to help survivors and clinicians access survivorship care plans.
  • Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, is a professor and co-director of the Aflac Survivor Program at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta and a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.
src 7 · Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies · hits 0/8 · bl 0 · emitted 8
EmittedMatched GTScore
The paper titled "Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies: A Systematic Mapping Study" was submitted on 8 Jan 2025 (v1) and last revised on 22 Aug 2025 (v3).(no-match)-
Chetan Arora is the author who submitted the paper on 8 Jan 2025 at 13:05:19 UTC.(no-match)-
The systematic mapping study reviewed 39 studies that discuss ethical concerns and mitigation strategies related to large language models (LLMs).(no-match)-
The authors analyzed ethical concerns using five ethical dimensions extracted from existing guidelines, frameworks, and an analysis of mitigation strategies and implementation challenges.(no-match)-
The study found that ethical concerns in LLMs are multi-dimensional and context-dependent.(no-match)-
The study concluded that ethical issues often hinder practical implementation of mitigation strategies, especially in high‑stake areas such as healthcare and public governance.(no-match)-
The authors reported that existing frameworks often lack adaptability to evolving societal expectations and diverse contexts.(no-match)-
The paper’s version history includes a second version submitted on 10 Jul 2025 at 11:51:53 UTC and a third version submitted on 22 Aug 2025 at 01:30:54 UTC.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies: A Systematic Mapping Study' conducted a systematic mapping study reviewing 39 studies on ethical concerns and mitigation strategies related to LLMs.
  • The systematic mapping study on LLM ethics analyzed ethical concerns using five ethical dimensions extracted from existing guidelines and frameworks.
  • The paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI' was first submitted to arXiv on 8 January 2025 as 2502.00015.
  • Chetan Arora is one of the authors of the arXiv paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies'.
  • Ethical concerns in Large Language Models are multi-dimensional and context-dependent.
  • Ethical issues often hinder the practical implementation of LLM mitigation strategies, particularly in high-stake areas like healthcare and public governance.
  • Existing frameworks for LLM ethics often lack adaptability, failing to accommodate evolving societal expectations and diverse contexts.
  • Mitigation strategies for ethical challenges in Large Language Models are complex and domain-dependent.
src 8 · Advaita Vedanta | PDF | Brahman · hits 4/12 · bl 0 · emitted 18
EmittedMatched GTScore
Advaita Vedanta is a nondualistic Indian philosophy whose name translates to "non-duality".(no-match)-
✓ Adi Shankaracharya formulated Advaita Vedanta extensively.Adi Shankaracharya formulated Advaita Vedanta extensively.1.000
✓ Advaita Vedanta posits that the ultimate reality Brahman and the individual self Atman are fundamentally identical.Advaita Vedanta holds that Brahman (ultimate reality) and Atman (individual self) are fundamentally identical.0.934
Brahman is described as unchanging, eternal, formless, and as pure consciousness (cit), existence (sat), and bliss (ananda).(no-match)-
Brahman is self‑manifesting, self‑illuminating, and is neither an object nor dependent on anything external for its reality.(no-match)-
Advaita Vedanta asserts the identity of Atman with Brahman as expressed in the Upanishadic mahavakya "Tat Tvam Asi" ("Thou art That").(no-match)-
Advaita Vedanta teaches that ignorance (avidya) veils the true nature of the self and creates the perception of separation between Atman and Brahman.(no-match)-
✓ Maya is defined in Advaita Vedanta as the cosmic illusion that creates the appearance of duality and multiplicity in the world.Maya in Advaita Vedanta is the cosmic illusion that creates the appearance of duality and multiplicity in the world.0.952
Maya veils the true nature of Brahman and projects the empirical reality of forms, dissonances, and relationships.(no-match)-
✓ Advaita Vedanta describes three levels of reality: Paramarthika (Absolute Reality) as the realm of Brahman, Vyavaharika (Empirical Reality) as the everyday world, and Prabhasika (Illusory Reality) as dreams or mirages.Advaita Vedanta distinguishes three levels of reality: Paramarthika (absolute), Vyavaharika (empirical), and Prabhasika (illusory).0.930
Liberation (moksha) in Advaita Vedanta involves realizing the non‑dual nature of Brahman through knowledge (jnana).(no-match)-
Practices such as meditation and the study of scriptures are said to help transcend ignorance and reveal the unity of Atman and Brahman.(no-match)-
In Advaita Vedanta, consciousness (cit) is the essence of Brahman, being self‑luminous, eternal, and transcending subject‑object distinctions.(no-match)-
Witness consciousness (sakshin) refers to the witnessing consciousness within the individual that remains untouched by the mind‑body complex.(no-match)-
The empirical self (jiva) is a reflection of Brahman distorted by the limiting adjuncts (upadhis) of body, mind, and senses.(no-match)-
Through self‑inquiry and meditation, the jiva can transcend its limitations and recognize its identity with Brahman.(no-match)-
Advaita Vedanta states that liberation involves overcoming Maya through knowledge (jnana), meditation, and direct experiential realization (samadhi).(no-match)-
In the state of realization, the distinction between knower, knowing, and known dissolves and one becomes one with Brahman.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • In Advaita Vedanta, the term 'Advaita' translates to 'non-duality', emphasizing the indivisible unity of all existence.
  • Brahman in Advaita Vedanta is described as sat-cit-ananda — pure existence, consciousness, and bliss.
  • The Upanishadic mahavakya 'Tat Tvam Asi' ('Thou art That') articulates the identity of Atman and Brahman in Advaita Vedanta.
  • Avidya (ignorance) in Advaita Vedanta is said to veil the true nature of the self and cause the perception of separation between Atman and Brahman.
  • In Advaita Vedanta, through Maya, the unchanging Brahman appears as the dynamic, ever-changing phenomenal world.
  • Moksha in Advaita Vedanta is liberation attained by realizing the non-dual nature of Brahman through jnana (knowledge).
  • In Advaita Vedanta, liberation is aided through practices such as meditation and study of scriptures, which help transcend ignorance.
  • Sakshin in Advaita Vedanta is the witnessing consciousness that remains untouched by the mind-body complex.

src 12 · Limits of Emergent Reasoning of Large Language Models in Agentic ... · hits 2/6 · bl 0 · emitted 9
EmittedMatched GTScore
✓ The paper titled "Limits of Emergent Reasoning of Large Language Models in Agentic Frameworks for Deterministic Games" was submitted on 12 Oct 2025.The arXiv paper 'Limits of Emergent Reasoning of Large Language Models in Agentic Frameworks for Deterministic Games' was submitted on 12 October 2025 as 2510.15974.0.925
✓ Recent work reports that Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) experience a collapse in performance on solving puzzles beyond certain perplexity thresholds.Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) were reported to undergo a collapse in performance on solving puzzles beyond certain perplexity thresholds.0.946
One potential confound in evaluating true reasoning of LRMs is the requirement that the model keep track of the state space on its own.(no-match)-
The authors provided a large language model (LLM) with an environment interface for Tower of Hanoi problems, allowing the LLM to make a move with a tool call, provide written justification, observe the resulting state space, and reprompt itself for the next move.(no-match)-
The authors observed that access to an environment interface does not delay or eradicate performance collapse in the LLM.(no-match)-
LLM-parameterized policy analysis revealed increasing divergence from both optimal policies and uniformly random policies as problem complexity increased.(no-match)-
The divergence suggests that the model exhibits mode-like collapse at each level of complexity.(no-match)-
The authors suggest that performance of the LLM is dependent upon whether the mode reflects the correct solution for the problem.(no-match)-
The authors propose that a similar phenomenon might occur in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs).(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The arXiv 2510.15974 study gave an LLM an environment interface for Tower of Hanoi problems, allowing tool-call moves with written justifications.
  • The arXiv 2510.15974 study found that access to an environment interface did not delay or eradicate performance collapse on Tower of Hanoi.
  • The arXiv 2510.15974 study observed increasing divergence of the LLM policy from both optimal and uniformly-random policies as problem complexity grew.
  • The arXiv 2510.15974 study found that LLM performance on Tower of Hanoi is dependent on whether the model's mode reflects the correct solution for the problem.

  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, acupuncture regulates and restores the normal flow of Qi, restoring health in the process.

src 14 · Disruption, empathy and transcendence: Why so many religions fast · hits 2/12 · bl 0 · emitted 10
EmittedMatched GTScore
✓ During the holy month of Ramadan, many Muslims fast from dawn to sunset.During Ramadan, many Muslims fast from dawn to sunset.0.951
In the year referenced, Ramadan coincides with Lent, when Catholics and Orthodox Christians give up certain foods, and with the 19‑day Baha’i fast that leads up to spring.(no-match)-
Sarah Riccardi‑Swartz teaches anthropology and religion at Northeastern University in Boston and says that creating taboos or restrictions around food makes sense because food is a universal human need.(no-match)-
Daniel Azim Pschaida teaches comparative religion at Gonzaga University in Spokane, Washington and explains that for ancient Mesopotamian Babylonian cultures fasting acted as a kind of repentance and that in Jewish tradition fasting is a form of teshuva, or atonement.(no-match)-
Catherine Newell is a professor of science and religion at the University of Miami in Florida and describes fasting as one of the only universal religious practices that disrupts everyday life.(no-match)-
✓ The Muslim Educational Trust in Tigard, Oregon hosts a nightly iftar meal during Ramadan where people gather for prayers and then break their fast together with dates, salad, rice and lamb.The Muslim Educational Trust in Tigard, Oregon, hosts a nightly iftar meal during Ramadan.0.934
Zikria Haqiqi, operations and communications manager at the Muslim Educational Trust, said the first week of fasting is the hardest because he experiences a pulsating headache, crankiness and hunger that later passes.(no-match)-
Amber Malik, director of community athletics and wellness programs at the Muslim Educational Trust, said experiencing hunger during fasting creates a higher level of empathy for people whose hunger is not a choice.(no-match)-
Jay W. Richards, a vice president at the Heritage Foundation and a Catholic author, said the abundance many people enjoy today is a gift but fasting provides an opportunity to reset both the body and the soul and to remind people of their mortality in the 21st century.(no-match)-
Fasting in the Islamic tradition is paired with charitable giving, and individuals such as Zikria Haqiqi have given up weekly game nights to spend more time in study, prayer, and reflection on the life of the Prophet Muhammad.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • During Lent, Catholics and Orthodox Christians give up certain foods.
  • The Baha'i fast lasts 19 days leading up to spring.
  • Sarah Riccardi-Swartz teaches anthropology and religion at Northeastern University in Boston.
  • In Jewish tradition, fasting is a form of teshuva (atonement).
  • Catherine Newell is a professor of science and religion at the University of Miami in Florida.
  • Ancient Mesopotamian Babylonian cultures used fasting as a form of repentance.
  • Fasting traditions are part of religious practices for Hindus, Buddhists, and Jains.
  • In Islamic tradition, fasting is paired with charitable giving.
  • Daniel Azim Pschaida teaches comparative religion at Gonzaga University in Spokane, Washington.
  • Jay W. Richards is a vice president at the Heritage Foundation and the author of 'Eat, Fast, Feast: Heal Your Body While Feeding Your Soul: A Christian Guide to Fasting'.
src 15 · Recovering from Microdiscectomy · hits 4/10 · bl 0 · emitted 16
EmittedMatched GTScore
Patients with a simple one-level herniated or slipped disc can quickly recover thanks to innovations in medical techniques and technology.(no-match)-
A microdiscectomy is commonly performed by a CNSO neurosurgeon or an orthopedic spine surgeon.(no-match)-
✓ Discectomy and microdiscectomy involve the partial to complete removal of a damaged or distorted spinal disc that causes patient discomfort or loss of function.A microdiscectomy involves partial to complete removal of a damaged or distorted spinal disc that is causing discomfort or loss of function.0.919
The difference between discectomy and microdiscectomy is the size of the incision and the tools used to assist with that incision.(no-match)-
For a simple one-level herniated disc, a minimally invasive microdiscectomy may be recommended with an incision no more than 2 centimeters.(no-match)-
Dilators may or may not be used during a microdiscectomy to maintain visualization through the very small opening.(no-match)-
A larger opening, as used with a discectomy, might be necessary when greater visualization of surrounding structures is needed or when treatment of surrounding anatomical structures is required.(no-match)-
Before a microdiscectomy or discectomy, the CNSO neurosurgeon or spine surgeon will thoroughly discuss the procedure with the patient, including preparation and postoperative expectations.(no-match)-
✓ Most patients may be able to go home the same day as the microdiscectomy procedure.Most patients can go home the same day as a microdiscectomy procedure.0.984
Patients with severe comorbidities may require a short hospital stay for observation after a microdiscectomy.(no-match)-
Most patients have immediate relief of their symptoms after a microdiscectomy, with only mild discomfort at the incision site that is relieved with minimal pain medication.(no-match)-
✓ Within six weeks after a microdiscectomy, a patient can resume all activities, although individual modifications may be needed and physical therapy may be scheduled.Within 6 weeks of microdiscectomy, a patient can typically resume all their activities.0.905
Most patients will be able to return to work within two weeks after surgery if their job is not labor intensive.(no-match)-
Providers commonly recommend limiting bending, lifting, or twisting movements during the recovery period to prevent re‑herniation and other complications.(no-match)-
✓ Studies cited in the National Institutes of Health Library of Medicine show that approximately 80% of lumbar discectomies achieve satisfactory outcomes.Approximately 80% of lumbar discectomies achieve satisfactory outcomes according to NIH Library of Medicine studies.0.957
Other studies report recurrent lumbar disc herniation incidences in the 5% to 18% range, while CNSO reports a 98% patient satisfaction rate and a recurrent lumbar disc herniation rate of less than 1%.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The goal of a microdiscectomy is to alleviate pressure on the spinal cord or on an aggravated, pinched nerve.
  • In a minimally invasive microdiscectomy the incision is no more than 2 centimeters.
  • Most patients have immediate relief of symptoms after a microdiscectomy.
  • Most patients can return to work within two weeks after microdiscectomy, assuming the job is not labor intensive.
  • Centers for Neurosurgery, Spine & Orthopedics (CNSO) treats patients throughout northern New Jersey.
  • At Centers for Neurosurgery, Spine & Orthopedics (CNSO), patients have a 98% satisfaction rate and the recurrent lumbar disc herniation rate is less than 1%.

Jain fasting includes complete avoidance of food or eating only a partial meal, eliminating rare or expensive foods, and avoiding sexual temptations.(no-match)-
The holiday of Paryushan, observed annually around August to September, is the time when Jains connect communally on the core tenets of the faith through fasting and studying.(no-match)-
✓ For eight to ten days during Paryushan, Jains focus on the values of forgiveness, humility, straightforwardness, truth, contentment, self-restraint, penance, renunciation, nonattachment, and celibacy.During Paryushan, Jains focus on values including forgiveness, humility, straightforwardness, truth, contentment, self-restraint, penance, renunciation, nonattachment, and celibacy.0.937

Chinese religious traditions, including Buddhism, Confucianism, and Daoism, visualize the refining of an individual’s character over a lifetime through fasting.(no-match)-

  • Mircea Eliade was a 20th-century Romanian historian of religion.

src 18 · Precision Medicine and Targeted Cancer Therapy · hits 4/14 · bl 0 · emitted 12
EmittedMatched GTScore
The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003 and decoded the entire human genome composed of 3 billion DNA base pairs and identified the exome that contains approximately 23,000 genes.(no-match)-
The exome represents only 2% of the entire DNA content of the human genome but encodes proteins.(no-match)-
The majority of disease‑causing mutations occur in the exome.(no-match)-
✓ In 2015 the US Precision Medicine Initiative placed cancer research and treatment at the forefront of medical priorities in the United States.The US Precision Medicine Initiative launched in 2015 placed cancer research and treatment at the forefront of US medical priorities.0.961
Herceptin is a targeted therapy that acts on the receptor site for human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her‑2) and blocks Her‑2 receptor function to prevent cancer cell proliferation in breast cancer.(no-match)-
Her‑2 targeted chemotherapy agents may cause cardiotoxicity, especially when used together with anthracycline chemotherapy agents.(no-match)-
✓ Pembrolizumab, marketed as Keytruda, is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD‑1 receptor on T‑lymphocytes and inhibits the PD‑1 pathway, leading to T‑cell proliferation and tumor destruction.Pembrolizumab (brand name Keytruda) is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD-1 (programmed cell death 1) receptor.0.910
Keytruda (pembrolizumab) has been shown to be effective against lung cancer, bladder cancer, head and neck cancer, renal cell carcinoma, and Hodgkin lymphoma.(no-match)-
✓ Former US President Jimmy Carter is receiving Keytruda for metastatic melanoma to the brain, and his brain tumor has shrunk to the point of being undetectable on imaging studies after treatment.Former US President Jimmy Carter received pembrolizumab (Keytruda) for metastatic melanoma to the brain, and his brain tumor shrunk and became undetectable on imaging after treatment.0.947
The average monthly cost for pembrolizumab at the standard dose is around $14,500, while higher doses used in clinical trials can cost up to $83,000.(no-match)-
Tumors with a high number of genetic mutations, such as those caused by tobacco or ultraviolet light exposure, are more susceptible to checkpoint inhibitor activity.(no-match)-
✓ Checkpoint inhibitors are monoclonal antibodies that bind checkpoint receptor sites on T lymphocytes, allowing the immune system to process and kill tumor cells.Checkpoint inhibitors are monoclonal antibody drugs that bind checkpoint receptor sites on T lymphocytes to allow the immune system to kill tumor cells.0.976
Missed GT:
  • The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003 and decoded the entire human genome composed of 3 billion DNA base pairs.
  • The human exome contains approximately 23,000 genes and represents about 2% of the total DNA content of the genome.
  • Herceptin is a targeted therapy used for early and late stage breast cancer that acts on the human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her-2).
  • Herceptin blocks the Her-2 receptor function and prevents cancer cell proliferation.
  • Her-2 chemotherapy medications may cause cardiotoxicity, especially when used with anthracycline chemotherapy agents.
  • Checkpoint inhibitors have been shown to be effective against lung cancer, bladder cancer, head and neck cancer, renal cell cancer, and Hodgkin lymphoma.
  • Cancer cells evade immune system surveillance through a process called immunoediting.
  • Tumors stimulate upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion and produce an immune tolerant environment via cytokine production.
  • Cancer avoids immune system surveillance by creating new surface proteins called neoantigens that are difficult to recognize as foreign, or by reducing antigenic proteins on the cell surface.
  • Cancers that are hypermutated can result from exposure to carcinogens such as tobacco and ultraviolet light.
src 19 · Seven-Year Experiment Uncovers New Insights into Nature of ... · hits 1/11 · bl 0 · emitted 12
EmittedMatched GTScore
A multinational team of neuroscientists tested the integrated information theory (IIT) and the global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) in a large-scale experiment.(no-match)-
✓ Integrated information theory suggests that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.Integrated Information Theory (IIT) posits that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.0.933
Global neuronal workspace theory suggests that a network of brain areas spotlights important information and broadcasts it widely when it enters consciousness.(no-match)-
The study involved 256 subjects, which is unprecedented for this kind of experiment.(no-match)-
The scientists presented the 256 subjects with various visual stimuli while measuring blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity in their brains.(no-match)-
The study found functional connections between neurons in early visual areas and frontal areas of the brain.(no-match)-
The findings de-emphasize the importance of the prefrontal cortex in consciousness.(no-match)-
The study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support integrated information theory.(no-match)-
The study did not find enough support for the idea that consciousness happens in the front of the brain as proposed by GNWT.(no-match)-
University of Sussex Professor Anil Seth said that no single experiment would decisively refute either theory.(no-match)-
The findings were published in the journal Nature in the week of April 30, 2025.(no-match)-
The article citation is O. Ferrante et al. (Cogitate Consortium). Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness. Nature, published April 30, 2025; doi: 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • A multinational adversarial collaboration tested two theories of consciousness: Integrated Information Theory (IIT) and Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT).
  • Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT) posits that a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely when it enters consciousness.
  • The consciousness adversarial-collaboration study involved 256 subjects.
  • Professor Anil Seth is affiliated with the University of Sussex.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study used three common brain measurement tools tracking blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support IIT, nor sufficient support for GNWT in the front of the brain.
  • The IIT vs GNWT adversarial collaboration was published as 'Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness' in Nature on April 30, 2025, with DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.
  • Identifying the neural origin of consciousness could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries, such as those in comas.
  • Convergence on a broadly accepted neuroscientific theory of consciousness would have profound medical, societal, and ethical implications.
  • The consciousness study's findings suggest the prefrontal cortex, while important for reasoning and planning, may not be central to consciousness itself, which appears linked to sensory processing and perception.

The study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.(no-match)-

Castellino, MD, MSc is a professor and pediatric oncologist at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta and researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.Sharon M. The research team followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016.(no-match)-
Outcomes were tracked for up to 10 years.(no-match)-
55.9% of the 3,366 survivors studied received a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
Survivors who received a survivorship care plan had a 38% lower risk of death over 10 years compared with those who did not.(no-match)-
SCP recipients were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes than survivors who never received a plan.(no-match)-
Survivors who were older were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
Survivors identified as non-Hispanic Black were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
Survivors with central nervous system tumors were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
Survivors with surgery-only treatment were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP developed Cancer SurvivorLink, a web-based platform for accessing survivorship care plans.(no-match)-

Missed GT:
  • The survivorship care plan study followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016 for up to 10 years.
  • 55.9% of patients in the Winship childhood cancer survivorship study received a survivorship care plan (SCP).
  • The Winship survivorship-care-plan study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.
  • A survivorship care plan (SCP) is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history and outlines recommended screenings, late-effect monitoring, and healthy-living guidance.
  • Childhood cancer survivors who received a survivorship care plan were less likely to die from relapse, treatment complications, or external causes than survivors who never received a plan.
  • Childhood cancer survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits.
  • Older childhood cancer survivors, those identifying as non-Hispanic Black, those with central nervous system tumors, and those who received surgery-only treatment were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.
  • Cancer SurvivorLink is a web-based platform developed by Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, to help survivors and clinicians access survivorship care plans.
  • Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, is a professor and co-director of the Aflac Survivor Program at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children's Healthcare of Atlanta and a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute.
src 7 · Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies · hits 1/8 · bl 0 · emitted 9
EmittedMatched GTScore
A systematic mapping study titled 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies: A Systematic Mapping Study' was submitted on 8 January 2025 and last revised on 22 August 2025.(no-match)-
Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed numerous domains by enhancing convenience and efficiency in information retrieval, content generation, and decision-making processes.(no-match)-
The systematic mapping study reviewed 39 studies that discuss ethical concerns and mitigation strategies related to Large Language Models.(no-match)-
The researchers extracted five ethical dimensions based on various existing guidelines, frameworks, and an analysis of mitigation strategies and implementation challenges.(no-match)-
✓ Ethical concerns in Large Language Models are multi-dimensional and context-dependent.Ethical concerns in Large Language Models are multi-dimensional and context-dependent.1.000
Proposed mitigation strategies address some ethical concerns in Large Language Models, but significant challenges still remain.(no-match)-
Ethical issues hinder the practical implementation of mitigation strategies for Large Language Models particularly in high-stake areas like healthcare and public governance.(no-match)-
Existing frameworks for Large Language Model ethics often lack adaptability and fail to accommodate evolving societal expectations and diverse contexts.(no-match)-
The paper was submitted by Chetan Arora.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies: A Systematic Mapping Study' conducted a systematic mapping study reviewing 39 studies on ethical concerns and mitigation strategies related to LLMs.
  • The systematic mapping study on LLM ethics analyzed ethical concerns using five ethical dimensions extracted from existing guidelines and frameworks.
  • The paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI' was first submitted to arXiv on 8 January 2025 as 2502.00015.
  • Chetan Arora is one of the authors of the arXiv paper 'Ethical Concerns of Generative AI and Mitigation Strategies'.
  • Ethical issues often hinder the practical implementation of LLM mitigation strategies, particularly in high-stake areas like healthcare and public governance.
  • Existing frameworks for LLM ethics often lack adaptability, failing to accommodate evolving societal expectations and diverse contexts.
  • Mitigation strategies for ethical challenges in Large Language Models are complex and domain-dependent.

Brahman is described as unchanging, eternal, and formless reality underlying the universe.(no-match)-

Atman refers to the individual self in Advaita Vedanta.(no-match)-

Avidya, or ignorance, veils the true nature of the self in Advaita Vedanta.(no-match)-

Paramarthika (Absolute Reality) is the realm of Brahman, unchanging and beyond time and space.(no-match)-
Vyavaharika (Empirical Reality) refers to the everyday world of perception subject to change and duality.(no-match)-
Prabhasika (Illusory Reality) encompasses dreams or mirages, which are subjective and transient illusions.(no-match)-
Jnana, or knowledge, is involved in the process of liberation (Moksha) in Advaita Vedanta.(no-match)-
Consciousness (cit) is the essence of Brahman.(no-match)-
✓ Sakshin refers to witnessing consciousness that remains untouched by the mind-body complex.Sakshin in Advaita Vedanta is the witnessing consciousness that remains untouched by the mind-body complex.0.916

Missed GT:
  • In Advaita Vedanta, the term 'Advaita' translates to 'non-duality', emphasizing the indivisible unity of all existence.
  • Brahman in Advaita Vedanta is described as sat-cit-ananda — pure existence, consciousness, and bliss.
  • The Upanishadic mahavakya 'Tat Tvam Asi' ('Thou art That') articulates the identity of Atman and Brahman in Advaita Vedanta.
  • Avidya (ignorance) in Advaita Vedanta is said to veil the true nature of the self and cause the perception of separation between Atman and Brahman.
  • Maya in Advaita Vedanta is the cosmic illusion that creates the appearance of duality and multiplicity in the world.
  • In Advaita Vedanta, through Maya, the unchanging Brahman appears as the dynamic, ever-changing phenomenal world.
  • Advaita Vedanta distinguishes three levels of reality: Paramarthika (absolute), Vyavaharika (empirical), and Prabhasika (illusory).
  • Moksha in Advaita Vedanta is liberation attained by realizing the non-dual nature of Brahman through jnana (knowledge).
  • In Advaita Vedanta, liberation is aided through practices such as meditation and study of scriptures, which help transcend ignorance.

  • Iqbal Akhtar is a scholar of religion at Florida International University.
  • Fasting is one of the five pillars of Islam.
  • In Islam, fasting develops submission to God, empathy with the poor, and repentance, and provides time for spiritual introspection.
  • According to the 12th-century theologian al-Ghazali, fasting can allow the believer to better perceive the ultimate reality of God as it involves all five senses — touch, sight, hearing, smell, and taste.
  • In Jainism, fasting falls under tapas or asceticism, which varies by degree between the laity and monastics.
  • Mircea Eliade described sacred time as lying outside of ordinary time, and fasting as one way to enter it.

Precision medicine incorporates the use of genomic information to guide medical decision making.(no-match)-
Targeted therapy is a type of cancer treatment that blocks cancer cell growth by interfering with targeted molecules involved with development of cancer.(no-match)-
✓ Herceptin is a targeted therapy that has been used in early and late stage breast cancer treatment.Herceptin is a targeted therapy used for early and late stage breast cancer that acts on the human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her-2).0.907
Herceptin acts on the receptor site for human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her-2).(no-match)-
The Her-2 pathway normally promotes growth and cell division.(no-match)-
When Her-2 protein is overexpressed on cell membranes, unregulated cell growth and tumor formation occurs.(no-match)-
✓ Herceptin blocks the Her-2 receptor function and prevents cancer cell proliferation.Herceptin blocks the Her-2 receptor function and prevents cancer cell proliferation.1.000
✓ Her-2 chemotherapy medications may cause cardiotoxicity, especially if used with anthracycline chemotherapy agents.Her-2 chemotherapy medications may cause cardiotoxicity, especially when used with anthracycline chemotherapy agents.0.994
Immunotherapy uses the body's immune system to fight cancer.(no-match)-
✓ Cancer cells evade the immune system's surveillance through a process called immunoediting.Cancer cells evade immune system surveillance through a process called immunoediting.0.991
Cancer cells reduce antigenic proteins on the cell surface or create new surface proteins called neoantigens that are difficult to recognize as foreign.(no-match)-
✓ Tumors stimulate upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion and produce an immune tolerant environment via cytokine production.Tumors stimulate upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion and produce an immune tolerant environment via cytokine production.1.000

✓ Pembrolizumab, brand name Keytruda, is a monoclonal antibody that binds to PD-1 receptor on T-lymphocytes and inhibits the PD-1 pathway.Pembrolizumab (brand name Keytruda) is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD-1 (programmed cell death 1) receptor.0.962

✓ Checkpoint inhibitors have been shown to be effective against lung, bladder, head and neck, renal cell, and Hodgkin lymphoma cancers.Checkpoint inhibitors have been shown to be effective against lung cancer, bladder cancer, head and neck cancer, renal cell cancer, and Hodgkin lymphoma.0.987
Yervoy (Ipilimumab) was the first checkpoint inhibitor released in 2011.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab is more tolerable than Yervoy (Ipilimumab).(no-match)-
The monthly cost for pembrolizumab at the average dose is approximately $14,500.(no-match)-
Higher doses of pembrolizumab used in clinical trials cost up to $83,000.(no-match)-
Immunotherapy does not work for all people with the same cancers or for all types of cancers.(no-match)-
Tumors with a high number of genetic mutations are susceptible to checkpoint inhibitor activity.(no-match)-

Melanoma and lymphoma generate a robust immune response and have shown consistent susceptibility to checkpoint inhibitors.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003 and decoded the entire human genome composed of 3 billion DNA base pairs.
  • The human exome contains approximately 23,000 genes and represents about 2% of the total DNA content of the genome.
  • Cancer avoids immune system surveillance by creating new surface proteins called neoantigens that are difficult to recognize as foreign, or by reducing antigenic proteins on the cell surface.
  • Former US President Jimmy Carter received pembrolizumab (Keytruda) for metastatic melanoma to the brain, and his brain tumor shrunk and became undetectable on imaging after treatment.
src 19 · Seven-Year Experiment Uncovers New Insights into Nature of ... · hits 3/11 · bl 0 · emitted 12
EmittedMatched GTScore
✓ A multinational team of neuroscientists tested the integrated information theory (IIT) and the global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) of consciousness.A multinational adversarial collaboration tested two theories of consciousness: Integrated Information Theory (IIT) and Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT).0.912
✓ The integrated information theory (IIT) suggests that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified, acting as a single whole.Integrated Information Theory (IIT) posits that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.0.948
✓ The global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) suggests that a network of brain areas spotlight important pieces of information and broadcast it widely when it enters consciousness.Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT) posits that a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely when it enters consciousness.0.945
The study involved 256 subjects, which is unprecedented for this kind of experiment.(no-match)-
Scientists showed study subjects various visual stimuli and used three common human brain measurement tools that track blood flow as well as magnetic and electrical activity.(no-match)-
The study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support the integrated information theory.(no-match)-
The study did not find enough support for the global neuronal workspace theory.(no-match)-
The study showed functional connection between neurons in early visual areas of the brain and the frontal areas of the brain.(no-match)-
The findings suggest that consciousness may be linked with sensory processing and perception rather than being primarily located in the prefrontal cortex.(no-match)-
The research findings were published in the journal Nature on April 30, 2025.(no-match)-
The study was conducted by O. Ferrante et al. (Cogitate Consortium) with DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.(no-match)-
The findings may help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The consciousness adversarial-collaboration study involved 256 subjects.
  • Professor Anil Seth is affiliated with the University of Sussex.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study used three common brain measurement tools tracking blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support IIT, nor sufficient support for GNWT in the front of the brain.
  • The IIT vs GNWT adversarial collaboration was published as 'Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness' in Nature on April 30, 2025, with DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.
  • Identifying the neural origin of consciousness could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries, such as those in comas.
  • Convergence on a broadly accepted neuroscientific theory of consciousness would have profound medical, societal, and ethical implications.
  • The consciousness study's findings suggest the prefrontal cortex, while important for reasoning and planning, may not be central to consciousness itself, which appears linked to sensory processing and perception.

  • The traditional Advaita Vedanta position is that Brahman is not an object that can be lost or found.

  • Traditional Advaita Vedanta asserts that a seeker must trust the word of the scriptures rather than rely on proof of Brahman.
  • Buddhist philosophy holds that everything is empty of inherent existence.
  • David Bohm's model of reality posits an implicate and explicate order.
  • Alfred North Whitehead's philosophical view describes reality as a grand interwoven process.

  • MHC class II deficiency, also known as Bare Lymphocyte Syndrome (BLS), is a rare combined immunodeficiency caused by mutations in genes regulating expression of MHCII molecules.
  • In MHCII−/− mice, impaired lymphostromal cross-talk in the thymus affects medullary thymic epithelial cell (mTEC) maturation and promiscuous gene expression and causes defects of central tolerance.
  • The article 'Thymic Epithelial Cell Alterations and Defective Thymopoiesis Lead to Central and Peripheral Tolerance Perturbation in MHCII Deficiency' was published in Frontiers in Immunology on 2021-06-15.
  • DOI 10.3389/fimmu.2021.669943 identifies the Ferrua et al. study on MHCII deficiency and thymic epithelial cell alterations.
  • Peripheral tolerance impairment in MHCII deficiency is likely due to defective regulatory T cell (Treg) generation and/or function and B cell tolerance breakdown.

Researchers at Winship Cancer Institute of Emory University and Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta led a new study.(no-match)-
Childhood cancer survivors who engage in long-term survivorship programs and receive a personalized survivorship care plan live longer than childhood cancer survivors who do not engage in long-term survivorship programs and do not receive a personalized survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
The study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.(no-match)-
The study provides the first evidence that survivorship care plans integrated into comprehensive pediatric survivorship programs are associated with significantly improved survival for childhood cancer survivors.(no-match)-
Sharon M. Castellino, MD, MSc, co-led the study.(no-match)-

Xu Ji is at the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center of Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta.(no-match)-
The research team followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016.(no-match)-
The 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors were eligible for a survivorship program at a comprehensive pediatric cancer center.(no-match)-
The research team tracked outcomes for the 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors for up to 10 years.(no-match)-

A survivorship care plan is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history.(no-match)-

✓ Childhood cancer survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits than other childhood cancer survivors.Childhood cancer survivors with multiple survivorship visits and survivorship care plan updates experienced greater survival benefits.0.986
Survivorship care plan recipients were less likely to die from relapse than childhood cancer survivors who never received a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-
Survivorship care plan recipients were less likely to die from treatment complications than childhood cancer survivors who never received a survivorship care plan.(no-match)-

  • The survivorship care plan study followed 3,366 childhood and adolescent cancer survivors diagnosed between 2002 and 2016 for up to 10 years.
  • 55.9% of patients in the Winship childhood cancer survivorship study received a survivorship care plan (SCP).
  • Xu Ji, PhD, MSPH, is a researcher at Winship Cancer Institute and associate professor in the Department of Pediatrics at Emory University School of Medicine.
  • The Winship survivorship-care-plan study was published in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute.
  • A survivorship care plan (SCP) is a personalized document that summarizes diagnosis and treatment history and outlines recommended screenings, late-effect monitoring, and healthy-living guidance.
  • Older childhood cancer survivors, those identifying as non-Hispanic Black, those with central nervous system tumors, and those who received surgery-only treatment were less likely to receive a survivorship care plan.
  • Cancer SurvivorLink is a web-based platform developed by Jordan Marchak, PhD, ABPP, to help survivors and clinicians access survivorship care plans.

Missed GT:
  • In Advaita Vedanta, the term 'Advaita' translates to 'non-duality', emphasizing the indivisible unity of all existence.
  • Advaita Vedanta holds that Brahman (ultimate reality) and Atman (individual self) are fundamentally identical.
  • Brahman in Advaita Vedanta is described as sat-cit-ananda — pure existence, consciousness, and bliss.
  • The Upanishadic mahavakya 'Tat Tvam Asi' ('Thou art That') articulates the identity of Atman and Brahman in Advaita Vedanta.
  • Avidya (ignorance) in Advaita Vedanta is said to veil the true nature of the self and cause the perception of separation between Atman and Brahman.
  • Maya in Advaita Vedanta is the cosmic illusion that creates the appearance of duality and multiplicity in the world.
  • In Advaita Vedanta, through Maya, the unchanging Brahman appears as the dynamic, ever-changing phenomenal world.
  • Advaita Vedanta distinguishes three levels of reality: Paramarthika (absolute), Vyavaharika (empirical), and Prabhasika (illusory).
  • Moksha in Advaita Vedanta is liberation attained by realizing the non-dual nature of Brahman through jnana (knowledge).
  • In Advaita Vedanta, liberation is aided through practices such as meditation and study of scriptures, which help transcend ignorance.
  • Sakshin in Advaita Vedanta is the witnessing consciousness that remains untouched by the mind-body complex.

  • Traditional Chinese Medicine originated over 5,000 years ago and is rooted in the ancient philosophy of Taoism.
  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, Qi (pronounced chee) is described as the body's life force energy.
  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, acupuncture involves insertion of very thin needles through the skin at points positioned along pathways called meridians.
  • Acupuncture has been shown to have pain-relieving, anti-inflammatory, hormone-regulating, and immunostimulating effects.
  • In Traditional Chinese Medicine, acupuncture regulates and restores the normal flow of Qi, restoring health in the process.
  • Meridians in Traditional Chinese Medicine have been shown in recent research to be real, anatomical structures potentially involved in tissue restoration and repair.
  • Chinese herbal medicine is typically prescribed as formulas rather than individual herbs, tailored to a specific TCM diagnostic pattern.

  • Iqbal Akhtar is a scholar of religion at Florida International University.

  • Jainism is an ancient religion from India that espouses nonviolence, nonpossessiveness, and pluralism.
  • In Jainism, fasting falls under tapas or asceticism, which varies by degree between the laity and monastics.
  • Mircea Eliade described sacred time as lying outside of ordinary time, and fasting as one way to enter it.
  • Confucians use the term 'ren' to describe a type of wisdom from fasting that translates as humanity, humaneness, goodness, benevolence, or love.
  • Daoism includes the concept of 'fasting of the heart-mind,' which involves detoxing the soul by controlling the five senses.

The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003.(no-match)-

✓ In 2015, the US Precision Medicine Initiative placed cancer research and treatment at the forefront of medical priorities in the country.The US Precision Medicine Initiative launched in 2015 placed cancer research and treatment at the forefront of US medical priorities.0.959
Precision medicine incorporates the use of genomic information to guide medical decision making.(no-match)-
Targeted therapy blocks cancer cell growth by interfering with targeted molecules involved with development of cancer.(no-match)-
Herceptin has been used in early stage breast cancer treatment.(no-match)-
Herceptin has been used in late stage breast cancer treatment.(no-match)-
Herceptin acts on the receptor site for human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her-2).(no-match)-
The Her-2 pathway promotes growth and cell division when functioning normally.(no-match)-
Her-2 protein overexpression on cell membranes causes unregulated cell growth and tumor formation.(no-match)-
Herceptin blocks Her-2 receptor function.(no-match)-
Herceptin prevents cancer cell proliferation.(no-match)-

Her-2 chemotherapy medications are similar to other adjuvant chemotherapy agents used for advanced breast cancer.(no-match)-
