Immunotherapy uses the body’s immune system to fight cancer.(no-match)-
The immune system identifies and destroys tumors even after early malignant transformation.(no-match)-
Malignant clones evade the immune system’s surveillance through immunoediting.(no-match)-
Cancer avoids the immune system’s surveillance by reducing antigenic proteins on the cell surface.(no-match)-
✓ Cancer avoids the immune system’s surveillance by creating new surface proteins called neoantigens that are difficult to recognize as foreign.Cancer avoids immune system surveillance by creating new surface proteins called neoantigens that are difficult to recognize as foreign, or by reducing antigenic proteins on the cell surface.0.937
Tumors stimulate upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion.(no-match)-
Tumors produce an immune tolerant environment via cytokine production.(no-match)-
Specific cytokines suppress immune responses and promote tumor growth.(no-match)-
Checkpoint inhibitors are monoclonal antibodies.(no-match)-
Checkpoint inhibitors bind checkpoint receptor sites on T lymphocytes.(no-match)-
Checkpoint inhibitors allow the immune system to process and kill tumor cells.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab is the brand name Keytruda.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab is a monoclonal antibody.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab binds to PD-1 (programmed cell death 1) receptor on T-lymphocytes.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab inhibits the PD-1 pathway that depresses the responsiveness of the immune system.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab activates the immune system with T cell proliferation, increased immune mediators, and tumor destruction.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab is effective against lung cancer.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab is effective against bladder cancer.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab is effective against head and neck cancer.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab is effective against renal cell cancer.(no-match)-
Pembrolizumab is effective against Hodgkin lymphoma.(no-match)-
✓ Former US president Jimmy Carter is receiving Keytruda (pembrolizumab) for metastatic melanoma to the brain.Former US President Jimmy Carter received pembrolizumab (Keytruda) for metastatic melanoma to the brain, and his brain tumor shrunk and became undetectable on imaging after treatment.0.914
Jimmy Carter's brain tumor has shrunk and is no longer detectable on imaging studies after use of Keytruda.(no-match)-
Similar responses to Keytruda have been reported in clinical trials.(no-match)-
Keytruda is more tolerable than Yervoy (Ipilimumab).(no-match)-
Yervoy (Ipilimumab) was released in 2011.(no-match)-
The monthly cost for pembrolizumab at the average dose is around $14,500.(no-match)-
Higher doses of pembrolizumab used in clinical trials cost up to $83,000.(no-match)-
Immunotherapy does not work for all people with the same cancers.(no-match)-
Immunotherapy does not work for all types of cancers.(no-match)-
Tumors with a high number of genetic mutations are susceptible to checkpoint inhibitor activity.(no-match)-
Genetically mutated cells stimulate the immune system.(no-match)-
Proteins produced by abnormal genes in tumor cells are more numerous and more abnormal.(no-match)-
More numerous and more abnormal proteins stimulate the immune system to react by binding foreign proteins.(no-match)-
✓ Hypermutated cancers result from carcinogen exposure like tobacco and ultraviolet light.Cancers that are hypermutated can result from exposure to carcinogens such as tobacco and ultraviolet light.0.911
Melanoma generates a robust immune response.(no-match)-
Lymphoma generates a robust immune response.(no-match)-
Melanoma shows consistent susceptibility to checkpoint inhibitors.(no-match)-
Lymphoma shows consistent susceptibility to checkpoint inhibitors.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003 and decoded the entire human genome composed of 3 billion DNA base pairs.
  • The human exome contains approximately 23,000 genes and represents about 2% of the total DNA content of the genome.
  • Herceptin is a targeted therapy used for early and late stage breast cancer that acts on the human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her-2).
  • Herceptin blocks the Her-2 receptor function and prevents cancer cell proliferation.
  • Pembrolizumab (brand name Keytruda) is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD-1 (programmed cell death 1) receptor.
  • Checkpoint inhibitors are monoclonal antibody drugs that bind checkpoint receptor sites on T lymphocytes to allow the immune system to kill tumor cells.
  • Checkpoint inhibitors have been shown to be effective against lung cancer, bladder cancer, head and neck cancer, renal cell cancer, and Hodgkin lymphoma.
  • Cancer cells evade immune system surveillance through a process called immunoediting.
  • Tumors stimulate upregulation of checkpoint inhibitors to promote T cell exhaustion and produce an immune tolerant environment via cytokine production.
src 19 · Seven-Year Experiment Uncovers New Insights into Nature of ... · hits 4/11 · bl 0 · emitted 28
EmittedMatched GTScore
A multinational team of neuroscientists tested integrated information theory (IIT) and global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT).(no-match)-
✓ Integrated information theory (IIT) suggests that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified for as long as the information is consciously perceived acting as a single whole.Integrated Information Theory (IIT) posits that consciousness emerges when information inside a system is highly connected and unified.0.965
✓ Global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) suggests a network of brain areas spotlights important pieces of information in the brain bringing the information to the forefront of minds broadcasting it widely the moment it enters consciousness producing conscious experience.Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT) posits that a network of brain areas broadcasts important information widely when it enters consciousness.0.920
Philosophers and scientists have sought to explain the subjective nature of consciousness such as the feeling of pain or of seeing a colorful rainbow and how consciousness relates to physical processes in the brain.(no-match)-
Various theories of consciousness evolved in parallel providing incompatible accounts of the neural basis of consciousness.(no-match)-
Empirical support for a given theory of consciousness is highly dependent on methodological choices pointing towards confirmation bias in theory testing.(no-match)-
✓ Convergence on a broadly accepted neuroscientific theory of consciousness will have profound medical societal and ethical implications.Convergence on a broadly accepted neuroscientific theory of consciousness would have profound medical, societal, and ethical implications.0.981
Authors tested integrated information theory (IIT) and global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) through a large-scale open-science adversarial collaboration.(no-match)-
Integrated information theory (IIT) indicates consciousness comes from the interaction and cooperation of various parts of the brain as they work together to integrate information like teamwork.(no-match)-
Consciousness according to integrated information theory (IIT) arises from how various parts of the brain are connected and how they share information with each other rather than any one individual area or part of the brain generating consciousness.(no-match)-
The study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support integrated information theory (IIT).(no-match)-
Global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) supports the idea that consciousness happens in the front of the brain.(no-match)-
The study did not find enough support for global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT).(no-match)-
The study involved 256 subjects.(no-match)-
Scientists showed subjects various visual stimuli.(no-match)-
Scientists used three common human brain measurement tools that track blood flow as well as magnetic and electrical activity to study brains of subjects while subjects looked at the visual stimuli.(no-match)-
The study showed functional connection between neurons in early visual areas of the brain and the frontal areas of the brain.(no-match)-
The findings de-emphasize the importance of the prefrontal cortex in consciousness.(no-match)-
The prefrontal cortex is important for reasoning and planning.(no-match)-
Consciousness may be linked with sensory processing and perception.(no-match)-
Intelligence is about doing while consciousness is about being.(no-match)-
The discovery has implications for disorders of consciousness such as comas or vegetative states.(no-match)-
✓ Identifying where consciousness comes from could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries.Identifying the neural origin of consciousness could help detect covert consciousness in unresponsive patients with severe injuries, such as those in comas.0.916
University of Sussex’s Professor Anil Seth said that no single experiment would decisively refute either integrated information theory (IIT) or global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT).(no-match)-
Integrated information theory (IIT) and global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) differ in their assumptions and explanatory goals.(no-match)-
Available experimental methods are too coarse to enable one theory to conclusively win out over the other.(no-match)-
The findings of the collaboration remain valuable with much learned about both integrated information theory (IIT) and global neuronal workspace theory (GNWT) and about where and when in the brain information about visual experience can be decoded.(no-match)-
O. Ferrante et al. from Cogitate Consortium published 'Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness' in Nature on April 30, 2025 with doi 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.(no-match)-
Missed GT:
  • A multinational adversarial collaboration tested two theories of consciousness: Integrated Information Theory (IIT) and Global Neuronal Workspace Theory (GNWT).
  • The consciousness adversarial-collaboration study involved 256 subjects.
  • Professor Anil Seth is affiliated with the University of Sussex.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study used three common brain measurement tools tracking blood flow, magnetic activity, and electrical activity.
  • The IIT vs GNWT study did not find enough lasting connections in the back of the brain to support IIT, nor sufficient support for GNWT in the front of the brain.
  • The IIT vs GNWT adversarial collaboration was published as 'Adversarial testing of global neuronal workspace and integrated information theories of consciousness' in Nature on April 30, 2025, with DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1.
  • The consciousness study's findings suggest the prefrontal cortex, while important for reasoning and planning, may not be central to consciousness itself, which appears linked to sensory processing and perception.
